Results of the source check below.

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text: Articles to be shown in *original language*: I just had intro/training with 7 APAC market teams for Harman and the only question they had was around being able to see articles in their original language. This continues to be a pain point in general for all non-English speaking markets and is a risk for future renewal of APAC and Latam, especially when English comprehension is low across some of these markets.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: Feedback from me — maybe nitpicky but did want to flag that the new search UI now requires two clicks to start a search. previously you could just start the search from the ever-present search bar at the top of page. it’s not a huge deal, but i do find myself already missing that small feature.

messages.author: Julia Gabriello
messages.text: Positive feedback from Fitch Ratings: :clapping:

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: Feedback/question for my understanding: We have a client that is expecting their keyword search to exclude any terms when special characters are in place.    For example, they have a keyword search stating "Arena Investors," but the results are bringing in articles stating "arena, investors" or “Arena. Investors” among the results.  Aside from entity training, is there something I'm missing or can we potentially look into getting more definitive into the way results are loaded as this can sometimes be a chore to explain to clients.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Vertex said they find the new search layout to be confusing and they prefer the original set up. specifically they said they're not sure where the filtering button (All of these, any, etc) went initially and said the Similar types is more straightforward than Match each term group

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from me: *Ability to track users' IP addresses to avoid seat sharing*
This has been flagged before, but seat sharing is hurting our profit and ability to upsell. Although we keep warning clients they shouldn't share login details, seat sharing is very common especially among agencies and a login can be shared with 10 people or more.
There are SaaS companies tracking users' IP addresses and would lock the account / kick users off the platform if someone is accessing from different cities / locations a few times per day.

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Teneo would like to 1) monitor reach per article, and 2) use the key story function to create a graph that shows the reach of individual story lines, by essentially grouping the same headline from different publications into one story and then calling out total reach of that story for a given day and given location

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: Another one for PayPal — they would like to be able to save searches with minimum reach as a hard filter. currently reach thresholds are only available as soft filters.

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal

messages.text: Not sure if this is a just a me thing, but it’s quite confusing that the “+Add” button only brings up the menu when you hover over it. When you click on it, which was my first instinct, nothing happens.

This meant that searches that were previously only RM+drones/lockers/delivery started bringing in 100s and 1000s of mentions related to Iran-Israel in their alerts. This resulted in their Head of Corporate Comms sending us a few angry emails. 
• What has been discovered through process is that it is basically impossible to pull change logs to understand which user made what change to what searches. I h

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text:
Hear me out....

Each time we have to create a user, we need to do the set up in NewsAdmin.
However, once that is done, we also need to remember to go to impersonate an admin and assign that newly created user in the web platform.

We often forget. When that happens, we create a "false start" for our customers: they want to log in, are given the keys to log in, but then face a blank Workplace-less screen. As if they were stuck behind another door.... not a great experience.

I would like us to be able to either
• assign to a workspace at the login creation in NewsAdmin
• stop us from being abl

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text: Second, we are also hitting a limit on *Premium Social alerts* per Workspace - there can only be 10 of them per Workspace. This isn't ideal for KPMG's Engine Room use case. Because of this limit, we are having to create Premium Social alerts across multiple Workspaces even if for the same monitoring purposes. The reason why there are so many is because of the All or Any operators. KPMG usually want to monitor social posts for themselves, their competitors, for many topics of interests and coverage. This makes managing Premium Social really challenging and frankly doesn't look great to the clie

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text: Hi Folks, we're coming across some feature limitations in KPMG Engine Room. First is *AskAIQ* and the *Schedule* feature limited to 15 scheduled threads and 5 daily scheduled threads per organization. These are all used up by ADP our partner for KPMG's RAG alerts. This means KPMG users are NOT able to benefit from the schedule feature at all. When you try to create one, they get a message saying they have hit the max but they can't see it in the shared Workspaces because it's in ADP's Workspace.

They are surprised that the feed that uses the Reading List doesn’t display the custom article.

Scenario 3: While

messages.author: Anwen Evans
messages.text:
Not a silly question at all, <@U060H6XDQES>!

For the past year we've used a tool called Sauce AI to aggregate feedback from Slack channels, Gong call transcripts, Support tickets, and Intercom conversations - enabling us to surface themes and segment by customer. We used this extensively for prioritising the UX improvements we'll soon be releasing - we looked for common issues for the Enterprise segment and all the feedback gathered in this channel was considered in planning the roadmap.

Sauce is actually winding down (the pace of change in AI SaaS right now!), so we'll be exploring alternat

messages.author: Abby Mills
messages.text:
I have a perhaps silly question for this process/flow: for <@UB0QCCUCU> <@UF907JYCF> maybe -- how is this customer feedback collected and prioritized? is there a threshold of like, we heard this request from X amount of clients so we are going to prioritize it?

There are a ton of good pieces of feedback here and recommended solutions and just wondering how it is all tracked :slightly_smiling_face:

messages.author: Abby Mills
messages.text:
*MetLife*/general feedback on *Ask AIQ*.

1. *Are there plans to optimize the format of the downloaded Ask AIQ report?* i.e., having this actually bolded like they are in the platform rather than be surrounded by asterisks? That will make these much easier for clients to share with senior stakeholders, and generally use more widely in their workflows.
2. *Are there plans to send Ask AIQ threads/reports via email rather than just workplace notifications?* This would allow people to get the report in their inbox, like bulletins, without having to sign into the platform, download a PDF, and then

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text:
do we have any plans to tackle this under the GRR initiative shared during demo yesterday? It falls under both the User Experience and Data Quality categories and it seems to be increasingly affecting more customers as publications restrict their content further, while our competitors are still able to offer at least headline mentions.

It would also be helpful for the commercial teams to have an enablement on why that is not possible in the meantime so we can pass that along in a client-facing way.

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: Meta would like the stories in bulletins to be summarised

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text:
Occasionally we are asked to merge accounts (individual instances on NewsAdmin). This often proved to be a gargantuan task for client services:

• Replicate Searches (including Exclusions, Feeders etc.)
• Replicate Bulletins (including readers, recipients, permissions, etc.)
• Replicate Reading Lists 
• Replicate Dashboards (Very difficult and time consuming, especially if there are many)
• God forbid: replciate tags and tagged stories.
It would be good to have a tech solution where we could migrate a whole workspace to a new Organisation or anything else similar.
<https://docs.google.com/spre

messages.author: Xavier Sedas

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text:
*Hilton/Harman:* Need to have linked reading lists.
Most of my enterprise clients now want to have reading T1 reading lists for local measurement programmes, but typically we will also be working with Global or Regional leadership for reporting both on and off platform.
In the below Hilton example, we used to only have a Pan-European reading list, but given my encouragement for them to increase Signal-engagement from country teams, we need to split the main reading list into country level lists, which will be at least 10-12 for Europe alone.
In the future, the country teams will be providing u

It would be a game changer for US customers to be able to pull coverage from specific states as well as cities.

messages.author: Michael Taiwo
messages.text: Diageo would like better *Reach* figures on the platform, comparable to their campaign reports. The platform numbers appear inflated (potential reach vs actual readership) and doesn't tell a realistic story.

messages.author: João Silva

Hi team, a client came through with this query:
"hi there - for some reason the bulletins i have set up are showing articles not from the last 24 hours, i.e. the same article will come on different days, or in the bulletin we'll see an article from even weeks ago"
How does the time span work for the briefing story content set in terms of what articles it will surface?

messages.author: Maria Terrado

In order to avoid reach fluctuations that does not reflect on the reality and are only the product of articles pulled by the platform (with reach numbers) and articles inserted via custom content (without reach numbers)

messages.author: Chloé Rodrigues

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Requests from *SAP APAC*:
1. *Ability to download and filter custom content*: e.g. they want to filter uploaded content by country or topic or user
2. *Ability to lock dashboards / searches / bulletins* so that they can just be viewable, not editable, for certain users to avoid deleting or editing without permission on a shared workspace (this has been asked for many times) 
3. *Expanding media coverage in China*, including content in traditional media, self-media, social media and phone apps 
4. *Arrange face-to-face meetings with users* - especially the ones in Beijing

messages.author: Osbert Diale-Abumere
messages.text: *'Coverage over time' feature enhancement request* - Our clients from <https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/wvp2gmk9/companies/65d862b03cb6f41eaedc715f|Verisure> want to see the second screenshot when they filter a search by multiple dates. Currently, we only see the second screenshot when we filter by a specific date. The client isn't satisfied with the first screenshot, which is displayed when the search coverage is filtered by multiple dates (and doesn't show the breakdown by time/hours).

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text:
*TikTok*: Dealing with a super demanding client who wants to 'automatically' remove any of their owned content (press releases/events) from their daily and hourly alerts. So even though I just go down to 7th Jan, the refresh bounced me back to 12 Jan, so I have scroll down to find where I am. It would be much more efficient workflow if the feed doesn't refresh until it is manually refreshed instead. So what should h

messages.text:
PwC wants to see full translated content for Regulatory sources.
The issue they find is that some sources' content can not be inspected in the web app due to the fact that this content set's behaviour will only display the first translated sentence in app and if you click through it'll bring you to the original document (website, pdf, etc) in the original language.
Since we're translating the content in full, having the translation in app (in an article viewer) would be beneficial for this client use case (and I imagine many others who are interested in Regulatory changes).

Allowing the most relevant articles to appear at the top by default would make workflows for ADP or others much faster, especially when writing newsletters. Constantly having to reapply filters is time-consuming and interrupts efficiency.

"_Unfortunately, our Australia team has experienced multiple instances of missed coverage, including articles from key outlets that were not surfaced by Signal AI. In one case, an article published on September 16th was only captured a week later via a manual Google search, and not at all by Signal AI during that period. Another article was missed entirely, despite a later syndicated piece being picked up. These

messages.text:
Just had a call with the *Freuds* team who had *overwhelmingly positive feedback* about our services - from content, to UX design and the overall features (awesome job team!!! :signal-ai:)  - On the improvements side they did highlight our reach numbers; I've seen this transferring to other clients who actively rely on the metric as well so thought it would be relevant to share the transcript below.

“_...I really like signal AI for using it for more quality, more sort of thematic analysis. I really like the aiq tool. I think it's fantastic. I find the tool super easy to use. I think the cover

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *Olam Agri* wants us to expand our content coverage in Africa (at least the English and French ones from Côte d’Ivoire, Nigeria, Senegal, Ghana, Chad, South Africa, Congo, Mozambique, Cameroon, Burkina Faso), adding that most titles in that continent are unlikely to have paywall so it should be easy for us to start scraping content from the websites. They also want us to offer more behind the paywall content for other publications too.

Ideally, they'd like frequent / realtime alerts during the day and less frequent / a single alert to access as soon as their online in the morning. *TLDR* the ability to have different delivery cadences for different time blocks in the day within the same bulletin (they aware of the custom option, but doesn't meet their needs) <@U0449M1SGTB>

Specific to Industry Searches that power Newsletters, Topics are not fit for purpose, when they are not paired with binary Concepts (Organisations, People, Substance, etc.). Most of the time, we have had to pivot to Keywords.

*1- Inability to use salience type filtering on topics*
For example "this story is very much about this topic, less so about that topic"

*2- Inability to use Prominence type filters*
For example, the topic matched with Headline / First paragraph / Full content

So if the results in a saved search are high volume, which they

*MetLife Korea* suggests us to report to our translation partner that their Korean-English translation is always incorrect, saying that the language is often translated to repeated English words without any meaning. Here comes the newest example:

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *Bain* - the same article can be flagged a few times and being treated as different stories on Signal because we get them from various providers at different times.
This is inflating the volume and causing data inaccuracy.  Bain says this happens very often and they cannot just choose to keep one version on the platform (if it's syndicated story). They flagged this issue repeatedly in the last 2-3 years and hope we can seriously look into this or the upcoming renewal in a few months will be at risk.

*Feedback from me:*

messages.author: Paggie Leung

Fresh feedback from *AIA Group’s main user Yvonne Kwok*:

Yvonne says she finds it “*very painful*” to use Signal to generate accurate data mainly because of *poor and unpredictable translation*. Even though we have recently retrained the *Ping An Group* entity, she finds articles which has no correlation with the company are often being tagged by Signal as Ping An Group’s articles.

Examples includes articles about “ping an buns”, which is a type of dim sum during a local HK festival in May, and also articles about someone reported safe (as ping an means safe and sound literally in Chinese).

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
We received more feedback from *MetLife.* This time from their *Japan team*. Though some of these were made clear to them before they signed up for the services, we are sure that these will be raised again when we will discuss the renewal later this year:

*1. Translation Limitations in the Japanese Environment*
Currently, Signal AI’s system processes Japanese-language content by first automatically translating it into English. However, the accuracy of this machine translation is limited.

Japanese terms often have multiple possible English equivalents, which can lead to incomplete or inaccura

messages.author: Will Wimberley
messages.text:
*Support Team request*: A QOL feature that would drastically speed up the completion time of larger workspace edit cases would be the ability to _*add multiple*_ keywords/entities when using bulk edit.

Currently you can add one keyword/entity at a time to multiple searches or folders but when there are more than one it generally works out faster to abandon bulk upload and just add them each manually.

In the long run this could shave hours off of workspace edit requests.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: No 10 would like if they could set up an email alert for a CSV download for a search of their choosing to be sent to them by email once a week instead of downloading directly from the platform.

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text:
Just flagging this bit of feedback as i believe this is desired behavior in place.  A user from *SAP* wants to use the reports feature to send all coverage from a search in a Report but "Select All" is only applicable to articles that are already loaded (20/20, 40/40, 60/60, etc.).  The endgame is to send a list of coverage out to a recipient base.  I've also had to explain this to another client when they were trying to send out a list of coverage based on an event that was held (I believe this was *Takeda*).
Is it possible for the "Select All" feature to actually select all articles, or is t

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text:
We’ve received feedback from multiple clients (*Paul Hastings and McKinsey)* expressing a strong interest in the ability to automatically surface names of individuals mentioned in coverage — without the need to train a people entity.

This is especially relevant for large law firms and consulting organizations, where thousands of partners are regularly featured in the news. Training individual entities for each partner, and their peers, is not a scalable solution (especially not dynamic as they move from one org to another).

Additionally, a new use case was raised by the *Northern Trust* Weal

I have a lot of unnecessary feeders like this which adds to complexity when training clients or trouble shooting isuses. <@UB0QCCUCU> <@U02G14PE4P8> we have spoke on this before.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong

messages.author: Melanie Downes

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: Feedback from me: It would be great if deleting a search could delete it completely from a workspace. What I mean by this is that, if we delete a search that is being used as a feeder to other searches, the feeder won't automatically be deleted. It will remain in that search with the note (deleted) blocking all the other filters from pulling any coverage. This "dead" filter/feeder is blocking the entire search that will be pulling 0 results because of this, when it should update automatically when a filter ceases to exist.

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text:
I have had regular issues with copying full workspaces into new accounts. AskAIQ can only be deployed across whole accounts, which means we have had to create whole new accounts just for select users to use AskAIQ (as it isn't permitted the whole org). HOWEVER, this is not the main issue I am trying to raise.
There is a serious limitation at the moment with the ability to colin whole workspaces - *dashboards, custom tags and bulletins cannot be copied over* and need to be _manually_ re-created. This is a huge time sink both for Support to manually re-create as well as for CS to check. Can we p

Zoopla / Alto would like the dashboard to be able to power at folder level instead of search level.
The Alto entity does not perform well,  so we need multiple searches to ensure we capture everything. Without being able to apply the folder at search level, we can only reactively capture every mention in the dashboard which the client is frustrated about.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *The Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels* would like us to improve our content coverage and translation quality, because they can only find 20 coverage in a search while their agency found 80 coverage during the same period. RWS has translated the alias of the company into “Grand Hotel”, which is one of the key reasons for the coverage gap.

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: One more for Abbott! They would like an easier way to see how Topic Analysis changes over time. The workaround that I suggested is to create separate analyses for each timeframe they want to look at (ie, Report - October 2025, Report - November 2025, etc), but this could get a bit clunky. What would be awesome is if you could change the timeframe at will in an existing report, like you can in dashboards, search etc. You can kind of do this by editing the timeframe and then running the analysis again but this does take a bit of time as you know.

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: Abbott would also like to increase the limit on the number of entities they can to a Topic Analysis :slightly_smiling_face:

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: Abbott is interested in using topic analysis to uncover previously unknown competitors/peers. Essentially wanting this feature to work the opposite of how it does currently where you put in entities and spits out topics, they want to enter a topic and have it spit out associated entities. Right now you can kind of do this with the landscape analysis within topic analysis, but you’re limited to the entities you already know about (since they’re powering the report). Let me know if you have any questions about this, not sure I am explaining it very well :sweat_smile:

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *SAP APAC* and *a few other clients (Especially those with shared workspace)*
• *Need:* The ability to see histories on the platform e.g. who built / last edited a search / bulletin / dashboards 
• *Problem:* People forgot what they have created or who created something, and they are hesitant in deleting things on Signal hence making some accounts very messy after a year or two. 
• *Outcome:* If they have the info, they can check with their colleagues internally instead of reaching out to Support for assistance or breaking something by accident.

*Feedback from various clients (e.g. Bain, SAP, AIA):* At least a few clients have flagged that CSV data downloaded from the dashboard charts can be different from the figures the charts suggest. Previously, we were told that such issue was caused by bugs on the platform. Today, Bain reported that the CSV file downloaded contains only 6k but the chart suggests 15k. Is there any solution to this? A lot of clients use dashboards for their KPI reports or for their database and accuracy is key. They *do not* accept discrepancies between the graphs and the CSV files.

It is a massive time to flag them because I know the scraping/translation issue is not a quick fix, but we really need the ability to remove entity and topic tags from irrelevant articles and would save me a lot of time from sending pointless tickets.

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text: *Mars Netherlands*: Would like the option to read bulletins in Dutch language, instead of translated in English which the user says is often poorly done. Exact words were "Sometimes I read the translated snippet and it doesnt look relevant to me at all. Then when I go to read the full article it's obvious why its relevant, but I don't always have the time to do that"

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: Feedback from me: Would it be possible to breakdown “Broadcast” on the *Account Management* page to show which broadcast content sets  (e.g. TVEYES Podcasts / TVEYES Australia) clients have access to? This may potentially reduce some Support tickets about why they can’t see e.g. podcast or Singapore TV coverage on Signal, and can be an opportunity for upsell too.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *MetLife Japan* wants us to improve our print (newspapers and magazines) as well as broadcast (e.g. NHK) coverage in Japan. Same feedback from *Nomura Japan,* *Takeda Japan* and *Mastercard Japan*.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Royal mail are missing a couple of key campaign mentions due to a high salience filter.
I gather high salience needs a score of 1 or more, but wanted to share some examples to explore two things:
<https://news.sky.com/story/new-ac-dc-stamps-to-celebrate-50th-anniversary-of-rock-bands-debut-album-high-voltage-13303580|This> had 4 mentions and one was in the lead paragraph.
<https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-14391753/Britains-romantic-streets-name.html|This> had 3 mentions and one was also in lead para.

1. We missed some mentions e.g. the caption mention because of what we cannot scrape,

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text:
*Topic mentioned in headline or lead paragraph:*
I have been asked to set up a search for Deloitte on global trade and tariffs, but given that the Trump tariffs are being mentioned in so many articles about specific companies or industries impacted by the tariffs, it is quite difficult to filter to what is irrelevant. For example in the below screencap below all these articles eventually mention that the new tariffs could impact these countries are causing uncertainty, but Deloitte only wants to know when the article is _about_ the tariffs changing. It would really help if we could have HL/LP

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *Bain* asks if Signal would regularly review whether our sources have been listed with the right source locations or have translation switched on. They found incorrect source locations on Signal from time to time which has messed up their regular reports, or they were told that translation had not been switched on at times when they shared some missing coverage.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: I've fed this back many times before, but I feel we really need a way to *tag an entity to Signal content* in the way we do to custom content. The use of custom tags can be confusing for less proficient clients often leaving this as a task for CS and support, and meanwhile it does have its limitations through impacting search results/ sentiment or salience availabilty.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: In addition to the feedback I shared by Omnicom last week, Royal Mail would like us to ensure we capture Byline mentions.

messages.author: James Nott
messages.text:
BYOC - Including "Reach" data
I thought that one of the key value propositions of the BYOC feature was to provide a comprehensive overview of media coverage for reporting purposes (particularly for dashboards) - and I expect that a significant number of our clients who receive reports (from our partners) would have Reach included on their KPI slide. My client Veritas has quarterly reports from Commetric which we add coverage to as we receive it from the client, we have all the data, and the reach figures, but can't get it into the platform, so the numbers are significantly off between what the

messages.author: RJ Zayas

Feedback from Mars in the US:

Overall, it’s been helpful for me in a pinch when I need to do some quick monitoring or coverage recap reporting. I mainly use it for issues monitoring via the bulletins and for times when I need to understand coverage/reach for something that our agency isn’t supporting us on (ex. our Hackettstown NYE event).

That said, it is not my favorite monitoring service or tool when it comes to user experience. For instance, I was trying to pull a quick report to show overall coverage and sentiment for Canadian stories about tariffs / products made in Canada for Tamar, b

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Clyde and Co*, being in the legal space, do not highly value sentiment metrics due to how often their positive coverage can be classified as negative given the nature of the conversations they're in (e.g greenwashing). They've asked if Topic Analysis could have a chart that looks at *association* only.
For now, I've told them they could just focus on just the X axis (e.g. how high or low on the chart they are) and disregard the Y Axis (e.g. how far left or right they are on the chart)

Still thought this would be worth while feeding back.

It isn't a great use case to have to build a dashboard just for _two_ metrics.

If there is a better way to 'bulk' provide access to multiple dashboards or share unique links only accessible to certain email addresses, that would greatly improve the 'reader' experience.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from various clients including *AIA Group*, *First Sentier Investment, MetLife* etc on exclusion:
• Take MetLife as an example: When they track news about the company, they want to exclude articles about “MetLife Stadium” (e.g. a match organised at the stadium).  But if they exclude “MetLife Stadium” as a keyword, articles that mentioned both MetLife the company and “MetLife Stadium” will be removed as well. Is there a way that articles that mentioned MetLife the company will still get included when it was mentioned alongside other unwanted terms?

Why? well if you’re building 18 searches  all for the same organisation but across two business lines, you could build it once, duplicate it 18 times and use the rest of the bulk edit function to to edit 9 of them one way and 9 another in the same amount of time it would take you make on complete search. messages.text:
*Anglian Water* have spotted a discrepancy in the numbers we are reporting in the Dashboard.

Simply put, from the 1st Nov - 30th Nov 2024

The Total Number of Articles we are reporting is - 857

However they have a Sentiment Chart that adds up to the following.
Positive - 138
Negative - 32
Neutral - 662
Sentiment total - 832

This shows a discrepancy of 25, which leads them to distrust the data we are reporting.

*Solution* - Can we please add a fix to the number discrepancy to the Dashboard work in 2025?

My solution would be to include an Email icon next to the actions button on the search, when you hover over it it would tell you which bulletin(s) the search is being used in.

Would also be useful to see a full list of searches that are powering the bulletins too, but i appreciate that's a bi

Feedback from *Nomura*:
A lot of Japanese articles would start with dates (e.g. On the 25th / On the 1st)  but RWS has been adding the wrong month to them. For example, there are dozens of articles about Nomura today but RWS translated the text to “March 25” instead of “September 25" or “On the 25th”.

They have a crisis today and unfortunately, many real-time alerts from Signal with wrong dates have been circulating widely to important stakeholders in the company.

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: Not ingesting content that is less than 128 characters is a huge issue for big clients. I am able to explain that a mention at the bottom of a paywalled article may not be picked up, but something that is mentioned in a huge European Newspaper where the mention of the organisation is in the *headline or lead paragraph* undermines our abilities as a media monitoring service currently. This is an issue for Clifford Chance, DP World, WPP and pretty much anyone else in my portfolio, but mentioning the bigger ones here. Is there a reason why we don't do this?

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
I'm finding that the NLA Print is 'generating estimate' even when the search hasn't been just updated. It says this search was edited 1 minute ago, but it wasn't - I just viewed the search.
Can we review a way for this to not generate an estimate when the search has only been looked at?

*Bulletin Request:*

1 - Could we add an option to include "Headers" or "Sections" when creating a bulletin? This would help in clearly separating different content purposes within a single bulletin.

2 - Is it possible to create an option that allows us to schedule a bulletin for internal review on the platform, instead of directly sending it to the audience? This way, key users can customise the "Newsletter" before it’s sent to the final audience.

happy to provide more details if needed.

B) Automatically redirect us to the *original publisher's website* when clicki

In a regulatory content context, the source location is pretty irrelevant as sources in any country could be writing about developments in another. For example, Canada passed a very interesting new tax recently that lots of countries are writing about, but what is more important to Deloitte is that the

messages.text:
Feedback from *HSBC Hong Kong*:
• *Need:* Please change “*China*” to “*Mainland China*” on the platform because “Hong Kong” and “Macao” are listed separately under locations and source locations, which would give the impression that Hong Kong and Macao are not part of China. Ideally, we should also use “*Hong Kong SAR*” and “*Macao SAR*” instead (SAR stands for Special Administrative Region). Not having this changed may limit their ability to show Signal data and graphs to their management or externally as HSBC could be seen politically incorrect.

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: *Feedback from me (possibly for Delighters week)*: In NewsAdmin for an inactive organization (lost or ex-client), visually it looks like we cannot re-activate a client.  In reality, this can be done by changing the state.  It would be great if a small change can be made to update the UX so support intervention is not needed for a commercial task.

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text: *Delighters week:* Would be great to add / exclude by 'Reach' (number or range..) when creating a search to get rid of all the low reach titles from results. Adding these sources to reading lists or individually to searches is time consuming and not scaleable.

messages.author: Virginia Lin
messages.text: Adding to <@UQD88CTQ8>’s comment above, I think this is particularly helpful since client won’t be able to anticipate how we have translated the headline in order to search for it.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *Feedback from SAP Korea:* The user hopes she can search a weblink in the search bar to find out whether Signal has a particular article. She thinks she would then be able to find “missing” articles easier without asking our Support Team to work on it.

messages.author: Jade Lloyd
messages.text: Hi! It would be very useful for the NLA online if you could have an enable all button so it would be quicker and not having to select them manually because I did this for a client. I thought of this because there is a disable all button which then lead me to think an enable all button would be useful. I just wanted to leave this feedback!!

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text:
The date range picker is quite confusing, even for people like me who have used it many many times. perhaps some UX improvement can be considered for the delighter's week.

by Confusing, I mean you don't know if you're setting a start or end date, when setting a date in the picker.

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text: A few clients KPMG, Ernst & Young, Eurasia Group have expressed the need to create an alert (via Bulletin or Reports) for Key Stories results. Is this something you guys are looking at? I have seen similar feedback in this channel. Key Stories are proving helpful but users don't have the ability to extract them quick enough to share with colleagues or their client etc. Am I missing anything?

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: ExxonMobil would like automated scheduled exports via feeds & dashboards (eg a weekly / monthly  excel & PDF delivery)

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Grosvenor* would like key stories to be powered by *custom tags*. This way for H1/H2 reporting he can take the summaries we produce and implement key highlights into his reports with a greater deal of ease.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *From me:* It would be great if for *custom content* requests we could hit a button in news admin as we do for LexisNexis to send the T&C's for someone to accept as its currently a very admin heavy job. Once accepted by a client, if an alert could trigger to the CS rep sp we can select the CC tick box in Salesforce, and also to Support to put CC live in the platform.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Marex* "_Re the mobile site, it would be useful to access the site and select the articles to include in the daily report and send the report out from a mobile phone – this is something we do early and it would be great to be able to do it from the train or from home without opening a laptop. At the moment on the site, the side bar is so large you can’t read any of the articles and it’s impossible to compile a report."_

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Praytell would like export links to be the article URLs itself vs masked Signal links

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: WE Comms would like ability to export soft filters

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: SAP Global would like an easier way to track sources and rename them as required; They are currently facing issues with their list of Hungarian publications as our internal naming can vary between the source name or its URL for some publications. We're unable to accommodate every single one of their renaming requests as this would affect other clients as well, so it would be great if we could have a feature that would be applied organization-wide, and that would allow clients to rename publications to what they'd prefer!

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Experian would like word clouds in the dashboard

messages.author: Mamta Bains
messages.text: Hi Team, it would be great to be able to see key stories in a dashboard view without restricting it to how many sources form part of a clients reading lists. In this particular instance, there were less than 500 articles over a given period but key stories would not display due to the number of sources across all reading lists powering the dashboard ( Target Media). However when I clicked on the "All Media" dropdown in the dashboard and the selected the target media lists, it seemed to work. I don't really understand the logic behind why it's set up in this way but it would be great if we coul

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: *Delighters week*: Ability to see Report open rate and recipients who open them. This will allow client to see the success rate of the curated reports and improve if need be

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: WE Comms would like access to Barron's

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: delighters: search option when choosing main anchor searches for dashboards especially for shared global accounts. it's a bit difficult to scroll and sift through searches when choosing a main search to power a dashboard

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: Delighters week: myself and someone else mentioned this before further up the channel but if the colour scheme across the dashboards could be consistent for each competitor in every chart that would be great. Rather than editing branding per chart, an option to edit per dashboard would be useful

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Marex & Lenovo would like a mobile app / more mobile friendly way to access and use the platform. 
Sometimes items need to be set up when clients are out and having the mobile version more accessible/ easier to use would improve user experience.

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: Small bit of feedback from me (not sure if this falls under Delighters Week).  Can we look to update the dashboard charts under "mention position?"  This visualization isn't used as much as others but consistency among the charts is just a small thing.  This is from a demo dashboard i was working on

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
*Delighter’s Week:* This may sound impossible but would be great if the relevant topics will appear in the dropdown even when users aren’t searching with the exact topic name / allow a topic to show when we hit certain keywords.

For example, a client asked us to train a new AI Topic for “Layoffs” or “Job Cuts” after she couldn’t find anything relevant on Signal. But we have indeed got the “Redundancies” topic. Should we at least rename the topic to “Reducdancies / Layoffs / Job Cuts” so that it would be easier for the client to find it?

Separately, I think there were also requests in the pas

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Delighters:* Grosvenor Group would like the topics in Topic Analysis edit bar to be listed *alphabetically* to make it easier to find the topic you're looking for when changing out topics.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: Coulson Partners really need our real time alerts to send as soon as we recieve content in the platform. On a few  occasions now, Coulson's clients are alerting them to coverage as opposed to the other way round which caused high risk to this account in March, and continues to cause risk.

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text:
<https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/578003e2-4c8c-47fa-ad2a-65c748ccfd60/web|BCW Global - US> would a column included in our CSV exports that identifies whether an article is premium content or not. Currently, all our CSV exports include article links with the "<https://article.signal-ai.com/>" domain in front of it; For Essential content, clients are redirected to the original website once they click on this while for Premium content, they are redirected to our platform.

BCW has been exporting coverage for their teams to do coverage reports which get sent to clients, and its

messages.author: Frederico Pinto
messages.text:
FEEDBACK BYOC:

Can we please add a dropdown selection when typing the source to add in the feature? this will allow clients to know exactly if we have the source or not and it will avoid mistakes when selecting a source.

It will also mitigate future issues for clients when trying to capture the article they have added.

Thanks

messages.author: Mabel Warnock
messages.text: *Delighter's week feedback:* The ability to group entities rather than make a new topic. E.g. If an organisation has a group of products it would be wonderful to make one search term rather than entering every product manually in the platform like "Takada's drugs" or "Mars' Products" - This could also help reduce the number or terms needed in a search

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Feedback from Openreach: ability to select multiple graphs in the dashboard and have a grouped AIQ summary

messages.author: Osbert Diale-Abumere
messages.text: <http://Capital.com|Capital.com> feedback: Allow Dashboard Charts to have filters for comparing data between (for example) Q1 VERSUS Q2 in a single-view display. This is quicker than using the filter to view data for Q1 and then toggling the filter to see data for Q2

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: *Delighter's week feedback*: Allow folders to power Breaking News section

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: Not sure if this falls within delighter's week feedback but I've had a few clients raise that it would be a game changer if we can show % change or benchmarking metrics for charts in dashboard. _Example: client inputs two date ranges, the figures will not only show the coverage volume, but also a (-/+ percentage change) next to the data._ This will allow clients to continue to find value from the tool without having exit the platform to calculate the change.

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: *Delighter's week feedback:* Ability to customize font style/bold/highlight text in dashboard.

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text: Salesforce would like an RSS feed for their workspace

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Delighter's week feedback:* If feeder feeds could remain inside a search if we duplicate it and also be given permissions to pull across to another workspace - that would be helpful for both clients and our CS + Support team who need to facilitate a lot of complex builds.

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Meltwater's homepage has elements of this and I've heard great feedback from clients on that UX

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: A bit larger than delighters week, but I definitely feel we should expand the home page in the web-app to update clients on key initiatives and events. not only will this ensure clients see the latest from teams like marketing, product and CS - but it can also be used to drive pipeline for new offerings like Risk. Additionally, we could then use bandwidth more strategically for targeted outreach rather than needing to contact every client for every update via email:

messages.author: Elaine Poon
messages.text:
*BYOC Custom Content Question*

Inability to add private BYOC sources to reading list is frustrating customers. Who is blocked by that?

The workaround is to add a custom tag to all custom articles eg “custom content”. Create a saved search combining reading list and the custom tag. This search can then be added to other searches.

How many customers are frustrated by this and why?

I know about RBC. I’m trying to understand the impact, as adding private sources to reading list is 3-4 weeks of work, which would have a big impact on the planned roadmap.

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Saeko from Nomura Holdings (Toyko HQ) would like to have a bar chart indicating % of sentiments by sources (i.e. bar chart with publications on the vertical axis and color-coded values ​​for each sentiment), perfectly in both DB and insight tab.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: *Delighter’s Week:* Allowing clients to adjust the width of the panel on the left so that more text can be displayed.

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text: Prudential Financial want to be able to identify op-ed/editorial content. I tried finding a key theme between the four articles they shared, but we don't appear to have anything that we could use in a search to accurately capture these types of articles

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text: Delighters Week: *Subfolders* on Web App. (Putting folders in folders)

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text:
delighters week: to be able to apply several filters (similar type/ any of those terms) onto different categories available in the "dropdown"
As an example: Any of those terms for AI organisations + similar type for 2 different reading lists OR
custom tag + AI organisations + media list - still use similar types
(to avoid the need of several feeder feeds)

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Delighters week: Download dashboard articles via CSV

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: Delighters week: to be able to update multiple searches with the same filter at the same time

messages.author: Will Phillips
messages.text: Delighters week: Regulatory content listed in the platform in the same way Print/Broadcast/Online is

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: Delighters week: To be able to add a reading list that uses more than one reading list into a new readinglist

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
*Finastra -*  They find editing the email directly in is hard, so they've been copy and pasting into an excel doc, which looks like the attached, and then have to remove all the extra spaces, and all the “view online / view on Signal” links because the title is already linked to the source, so it’s a bit unnecessary. They then copy and paste this back into an email to send to the wider business.

If there was any more customisation that can be provided in Bulletins then Finastra would be happy to provide some feedback on what would work well for them.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Lenovo* would highly value a chart in the insights tab that priorities top sources by highest potential *reach* as opposed to highest volume of articles.

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: Feedback from McKinsey Brazil: they would like the platform to see the content surfacing in Signal made available in both English and in the original language in which it was published. I figure they would like to see some sort of option through which they could switch back and forth between English language and original language.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from me on RWS:

Not sure if this should be flagged here: Just noticed that some Japanese-language articles ( <https://newscollect.jp/article/?id=1163417253161288537&tid=14|Example 1>, <https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/item/o/1211605/|Example 2>, <https://news.biglobe.ne.jp/sports/0517/jj_240517_8164200633.html|Example 3>, <https://www.excite.co.jp/news/article/Jiji_3238736/|Example 4>, <https://www.mapion.co.jp/news/release/000000192.000035073/|Example 5>) have been marked as Chinese on the platform. How can we correct them? Not sure if we can correct the language for these sources

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text: Delighter's week feedback: The ability to add Custom Content to specific sources that already exist within the platform _(similar to how the adding a source to a search works),_ if the source doesn't exist in Signal then a tick box to confirm it is a fully new source _(or something similar)_. This will ensure that clients can know for a certainty that their coverage is going to be in the source they have selected rather than the potentially convoluted way that is currently live.

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text: Keep those great ideas coming, Mel!

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Delighter's week feedback:* To be able to 'copy to' an individual search from a client workspace over to a Partner workspace in the same way we can from one client workspace to another client workspace.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: feedback from me - can we show content readers in newsadmin? Fabiano is a reader under <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/cfb781af-603c-461d-9ac6-f618f254b089/web|Mars Wrigley (US & APAC & MEA)>

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Experian would like the ability to set and lock colors in Topic Analysis. Every time they switch the dates, the colors assigned to each company change so using this data to track changes overtime isn't visually successful:

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Delighter's week feedback:* 
*Tagging AI entities to Signal content* *in the exact same way we do for custom content.* 
This is something that would significantly change how easily CS and Support can help clients with entity misses as we wouldn't need to escalate to concept ops for retro tagging. In addition, it would likely reduce tickets as its potentially much easier for clients to logically include missed coverage without having to create custom tags. Not only that, it will inform all of their charts where tags do not. This could quite possibly be a bigger task than delighter's week allow

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: Feedback from BCG: They would like to have a list of articles displayed in dashboards without having to click on any charts.

messages.author: Atiana Salazar
messages.text: Feedback from GM: They would like to be able to track analysts of their choosing quoting them and competitors of their choosing. For now we have quote sentence on which they are okay with but it doesn’t guarantee analysts quoting them directly.

messages.author: Charlie Gibson
messages.text:
Feedback from Depop:
The auto-transcript that we receive from TV Eyes is not reliable & damages the trust they have for the Signal platform. Big pieces of coverage being missed because the auto-transcript didn't correctly tag something is a problem for them as 'Depop' has been missed on numerous occasions. If there was a feedback forum or method for them to navigate this it would have a big impact on how much they can rely on and trust the Signal platform.

messages.author: Ricardo Soares
messages.text: Feedback from SAP: Have the original URL for the article in the CSV exports as individuals without reader access aren't able to access the current Signal links.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Man Group shared alot of very valuable feedback today:* 

1. *Advanced Analytics*: They are impressed with the visuals and possible insights of Advanced Analytics, however, they sometimes struggle to trust our data quality, specifically around sentiment, which they fear will impact the reliability of the insights. He asked if we could improve our technology around sentiment rankings as often their PM's are mentioned in coverage that could be discussing a negative topic, but it is actually favourable to Man Group. 
2. *Custom Content:* He would Man group to be able to tag a *high/low salience

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: _" Hi Guilherme, thanks for the quick response. I understand. Could you please share my feedback with the team that overall, the left sidebar sometimes feels unresponsive and slow (e.g., when deleting searches, creating new ones, creating a folder, moving a search to a different folder, etc.)? It would be great if performance improvements could be considered. This may be due to our workspace having a lot of searches as well. Thanks and have a a great day! "_

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: _"Hello, is there a way to delete searches faster, for example in bulk? The way I currently delete searches feels slow, sluggish and sometimes unresponsive (through the context menu for a search on the left, click delete and confirm in the popup)."_

messages.text: Quick note from Meta Global

messages.author: Guilherme Gonçalves
messages.text: Hey Team!

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Cognito would like our archive to extend to 36 months of data

messages.author: Jackie Kirwan
messages.text: Feedback from Takeda of having the ability to add the names of chemicals in different languages (native to their own).

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Grosvenor Group:* To be able to tag an AI entity to Signal coverage when we have entity misses.  (raised before) :slightly_smiling_face:
