messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Here is a feedback from Takeda's training session (APAC): they would like to monitor *keywords* mentioned (in *original lang*) in articles, e.g. which keywords were always mentioned, etc.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Mckinsey would like to see the full list of sources when looking at soft filters

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
Request from me regarding *News Admin.* 

Can we please remove deactivate users from the Platform User list?

It gets a bit confusing understanding how many users are _actually_ active as the number next to platform users includes the deactivated users. Also, it can be a pain to scroll and find the right user amongst all the inactive & greyed out  ones

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Kindred Ventures would like the ability to automatically sort searches within a folder by alphabetical order

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text: Hi Guys.  I am using Key Stories for Entity: KPMG, and Source Location: Spain. However, it's showing me results from publications outside of Spain. Is that normal? I thought Key Stories supported Source Location.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *Takeda Japan*’s *Head of Comms Tsuyoshi Tada*:

********
“_While I understand it is difficult to exclude, there are so *many noises in the search results for Takeda Pharmaceutical Company.*_

_As I mentioned before, ‘Takeda’ is very common family name in Japan, so there’s millions pattern of person’s name who has ‘Takeda’ in its family name. Unfortunately, the current search results include such names and I believe the ‘bulked-up’ results are reflected in the dashboard. I this situation, *we cannot use the dashboard as an indicator of our activities*._

_I really hope you could

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *Mars China*:
1. They couldn’t watch the videos on Signal Academy because they couldn’t access YouTube from China - and Mars blocks them from using VPN. They were asking if we had the vidoes available elsewhere.
2. They hope the email bulletins can show summaries in the original language - rather than in English as it would be easier to read without translation issues.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Feedback from me* - It would be invaluable to be able to t*ag AI entities to Signal content* to remove the need for custom tags in more cases as it causes a big manual burden for clients who's charts are not correct because a portion of their coverage is tagged.

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <http://Chartered%20Institute%20for%20Securities%20&%20Investment%20(CISI)|CISI> would like a pie chart in their dashboard to specify all of the countries included in the "Other" slice of their Target Media graph. If we're sticking with a 10 at a time limit, I guess it would also be useful for clients to let them choose which countries they want to see reflected in what slice (much like that is somewhat available in the "Comparison" tab when looking at a folder level results).

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text:
Petco would like access to Gray TV for
    ◦ KMOV – <http://kmov.com/> (UVM: 1.1M; DMA rank 21)
    ◦ KPHO – <https://www.azfamily.com/> (UVM: 2M; DMA rank 11)
    ◦ WOIO – <https://www.cleveland19.com/> (UVM: 1.5M; DMA rank 19)

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Frontier Communications would like access to KWQC-DAV (NBC) that's licensed by Gray TV

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: The Wonderful Company would like access to Gray TV for WANF

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/ae006943-4ae5-4e33-9500-e81e4399b753/web|Freuds> would like the ability to sort articles in their search results by reach; A current workaround is to download the search in question as a CSV and analyse/reorganise the results by reach number from there, which is not that practical for clients.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
*Feedback from Bain APAC’s Shelly*: “We’ve noticed that Signal treats ME as a region under Asia. Is there a way to change this to be included under Europe?”

(This is because Central Asia and Western Asia, including countries like UAE, Kazakhstan are also included under “Asia” the Location / Source Location on the Signal platform, but people in general may not count Middle East as part of Asia. Understand this can be an international / UN definition issue and we may not be able to do anything. But am flagging it here as some users / Signallers may not know).

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: feedback from me - it'd be great if users can manage login switches on their end. currently support needs to note the start and end date for swapping users in these instances

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text:
Mastercard would like SOV by Competitor overlaid with Sentiment in the dashboard. I've had many other clients request this as well, especially as we already do SOV by competitor overlaid with topics, which would be the same build structure.

The reasoning: "The extended team would like to have this view, so they can provide context to SoV, which may at times be higher for a competitor, but not necessarily favorable/ positive. Thanks!"

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Citi would like the ability to schedule auto-send dashboards for readers. For example, they build a global measurement dashboard that then automatically gets sent every month to their readers.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: and another:

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: another example of incorrect sentiment labelling:

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: Unfortunately Polestar are still flagging major issues with our sentiment, so much so it's been escalated to their CCO. <https://www.notebookcheck.info/O-conceito-Polestar-8-lanca-um-olhar-para-o-futuro.829211.0.html|This> article was marked as "negative" for Polestar 3 product, by AI marking "Although the Polestar 3 should become the brand's flagship" as a negative sentiment sentence.. which is not correct- in fact, the article is indeed VERY positive for the product and the client..

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/27b0851b-0521-48dc-bd73-b6cd4767e44e/web|Deloitte US> would like to have more insights on what reporters are specifically writing stories; Having the information available in a CSV export instead of the story card itself would also be helpful to them.

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text:
Hello hello.  Just finished a dashboard training session with *Praytell* with some suggestions, mostly cosmetic (some may be old suggestions):
• Changing the fonts on the dashboard
• Possibility to add static text over visualizations
• YOY comparison (as illustrated).
• Possibility to add arrows to point out any drastic change in coverage.
The idea behind these suggestions is to see where they can rely less on external applications (spreadsheets, Tableau) to help with reporting so they can use Signal.

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Experian would love if we could send Key Stories in reports. Kelsey feels senior team members would greatly benefit from alerts or reports using this feature as it shows the weight or impact of a specific story rather than seeing every article come through

messages.author: James Nott
messages.text:
*Client*: Competition & Markets Authority
*Request*: Checking to see whether or not we can implement a process where we _highlight the passage in the article_ that determines whether an article is being tagged as positive or negative sentiment
*Benefit*: Clients can then have more insight as to how the sentiment has been determined within the body of the article and provide constructive feedback

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/2b8c934f-06de-459d-99d7-530c400d4bb4/web|Wootech Limited> would like the Advertising Value Equivalency based on the media identified by Signal to be a feature available on the platform.

messages.author: Frederico Pinto
messages.text:
*My feedback:*
It would be highly beneficial for Signal AI to have an option in the dashboards to measure Spokespeople Vs topics.
Similar to what we do with competitors Vs key topics.
Is this something we are looking to add?

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: FIS finds AIQ summaries very impactful, however they want the AIQ summaries to share what is driving the coverage can causing a spike in coverage or what is causing a competitor to have the majority of coverage such as earnings news or a recent influx of news surrounding a particular topic

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Grosvenor Group and Gong:*
When adding custom content, rather than just typing in a source name, please enable clients to tag an existing Signal Source when relevant (to their environment only) to ensure it informs the clients dashboards without the need for use of custom tags.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Salesforce is struggling to load their SFDC global dashboard and have flagged platform performance issues

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: The current behavior of timezone for CSV exports being based on the workspace location while search content itself being browser-based is causing some confusion for <http://Takeda%20Media%20Monitoring|Takeda>; It would be great if we could standardize this, as they currently have a few workspaces set to London timezone with users based across Europe and US.

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Experian would like the AI to auto pull spokespeople from coverage rather than them needing to proactively search for a spokesperson's coverage via keywords

messages.author: Charlie Gibson
messages.text:
Some feedback from me, and I am sure this may have been mentioned before. It's about limits on how many workspaces, reader seats etc that customers can setup.
It would be great if the cap/limit that we put into News Admin actually blocked users from being able to exceed their allowance. For example if a client only has an allowance of 4 workspaces don't allow them to create a 5th one. If they only have an allowance of 5 reader seats don't let them setup a 6th one and so forth.

messages.author: Jacob Flaxman
messages.text: A note on the above too - can we expand the news feed to be able to draw from a folder and not just saved searches, please? Clients might create a topic folder and want the live newsfeed to draw from all the topic searches, not just one.

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Feedback from Centrica: New home page has too much white space, new UI is not aesthetically pleasing. I told them it was so we’d have space for more widgets in the home page - please can we accelerate this?

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: Not sure if this is worth "escalating" but *Magnitude Growth* noticed a visual discrepancy between content on the platform and content added via "Custom Story" when using the report feature, when trying to keep things uniform when sending out

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text:
InEight would like the following features to be implemented into the platform:
• The ability to choose their own definition of high and low salience. They are a smaller company competiting with organizations who have a broader presence. Therefore, their high salience results are very different to what their competitor might receive
• A numerical impact score that they can assign to each article within the platform to allow them to identify the value of their communication efforts week over week, month over month, and quarter over quarter.

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text: *Amnesty* told me that they just ignore the Sentiment part of the platform now because its frequently incorrect. They directed me to this recent post that is essentially a hit piece calling them a Sham that we deemed was Positive.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
MHP said it would be helpful if we could recognise capital letters. E.g.
They're trying to review the coverage for an org called *Grow Indigo*, however, the system brings in lots of non-relevant coverage around '*grow indigo'* (lowercase) which is about growing indigo, rather than the company

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Victor from Interactive Investor would like to see a more accurate transcript in broadcast

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: <http://Monday.com|Monday.com> would like if we could partner with <https://ifat.co.il/product/%D7%A0%D7%99%D7%98%D7%95%D7%A8-%D7%95%D7%9E%D7%99%D7%93%D7%A2-%D7%AA%D7%A7%D7%A9%D7%95%D7%A8%D7%AA%D7%99-2/|Ifat infor> for greater access to Isreal contnet.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: Platform seems to have issue recognising specific symbols such as "%" causing difficulties tracking certain topics/ press releases. It would be great if we could recognise that symbol correctly and it would solve problems experienced by the likes of Polestar etc.

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: ORPG would love to see us use AI to analyze sentiment via *tone of voice* in Podcast/Broadcast coverage. They specifically called out the desire to understand when sarcasm is used as well as the sentiment with which a mention was given.

messages.author: Andre Basilio
messages.text: Andy Lane of <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/fc0c1e23-d67d-4d7d-b680-50288c6487da/web|Instinctif Partners> has requested that a weekly/monthly usage update for NLA Print is sent to platform admins with actual usage vs. period allowance.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: it would be super useful if we would download the names and access level details of recipients from the account management page. At the moment the only option is to switch the nla on/off:

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: Feedback from me and partners: it is now becoming essential to have NEAR operators in the platform for accurate analysis. A lot of our clients are using our services to assess whether they are hitting their reputational pillars. This is done by using searches that combine AI trained topics and keywords in conjunction with the organisation entity. This does not guarantee that the topic or keyword is actually associated with the organisation, just that they are mentioned in the same article. Currently this is being somewhat overcome by using saliency on an org level, but this is still not a grea

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Prosek would like notes next to inactive/dead sources

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Feedback from me:
When we change branding in the dashboard for one chart, it would be handy for this to be applied to all charts where the same search is powering the branding change.
Example below.

messages.author: Lea Tumale
messages.text:
Recurring Eurasia Group feedback: EG use Signal on behalf of one of the biggest clients Tata (specifically the Strategy team). Last year they shared the feedback below and would like to resurface as it will improve the uptake of Reports and plan to scale for Tata and potentially other clients. Have a look at some of their sent Reports as they are interesting risk reports sent to Tata.
• Reports formatting: the ability to add hyperlinks to text, and embed graphics to summaries/text without having to create a different section on the Report. 
• Bulletins: Ability to add articles from Bulletins i

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Feedback from me:
Do we plan to add 'add to report' functionality for the key stories articles?

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: WeWork would like the ability to select lead articles to be considered in their dashboards/ ability to remove syndicates without a reading list

messages.author: Andre Basilio
messages.text: I think this might've been raised here before but: the possibility of copying bulletins from one workspace to another.

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Feedback from Hogan Lovells: Please can we highlight articles that have been tagged (eg by a little emoji visible at the article list level (rather than having to look into an article card). This will streamline their tagging efforts as multiple people on the team tag in different timezones - locating which articles need to be tagged will be much easier. Pegasystems have also requested something similar

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*My Feedback:* I've raised this before but it would be amazingggg if we could *tag an AI entity to Signal Content* in the same way we can for custom content to reduce the need for custom tags + ensure we can include any entity misses in Signal insights.

_(It would also be very good if when we click 'no, this article isn't about this entity' that it removes it from the entity as most clients have the misconception that this automatically informs the AI)._

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/745d6ca5-480e-45f7-aeb5-6fbe2ce37662/web|SAP GLOBAL> would like for the author's name of their respective articles to be displayed on the platform, as their organization/ someone they are looking to track is often mentioned in that section.

messages.author: Andre Basilio
messages.text:
*What:* A lock function/button on searches in the web app
*Why:* Due to a number of workspaces being shared by dozens of users, some searches are changed while no visibility/traceability is offered of the terms powering a search - this has resulted in circular feeds and unexpected and irrelevant coverage
*Who:* Customer Support Team :slightly_smiling_face:

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text: This is not all that helpful if a client, a CSM or Support are uploading a big CSV of sources for a reading list.

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
*Reading List CSV Uploads Needs to be improved.* 

It's quite frustrating when we upload a large CSV folder to a reading list as if they don't match exactly then it will simply say X number of sources failed to upload. It is then up to the CSM / Support team to go through that list and make sure that we are either adding them in manually or checking if we have them.

I've seen a prototype that was worked on a while ago with <@U03QDPVSSA3> & <@U047L4HECGN> that could solve this problem & could also help when it comes to source checks as well. Can this please be re-opened as I'm certain

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text: Reiterating the desire for "search term Upload": a prospect has 300 Spokespeople and as of now, we need to create 300 saved searches manually. Being able to eventually upload such search terms would be beneficial to our efficiency goals.

messages.author: Max Tam
messages.text:
Hello happy monday!

I recall feedback about searching within a saved search feed, but I can’t locate the specific comments here/productboard.

*Would enabling users to search the result of a saved search (e.g., with ad-hoc search terms, in addition to facet filters) be beneficial?*

It would be helpful to have quotes, anecdotes, or use case examples.

Thanks :pray:

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/745d6ca5-480e-45f7-aeb5-6fbe2ce37662/web|SAP GLOBAL> would like for articles that are retroactively added to a search via a custom tag to be included in the next bulletin to be sent out after the change

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *Advanced Navigation:* 
They want our entities to be case sensitive e.g. picking up “*A*dvanced *N*avigation” instead of “advanced navigation” which will increase the accuracy of entities.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Feedback from Number 10* - We tend to recommend to our clients to divide by 30 when looking at reach data for daily stats. Number 10 would like us to have the option automatically do this division in the web app to save the manual need to do this offline.
This would improve user experience and reduce a manual step that I imagine most of our clients otherwise need to do.

messages.author: Chloé Rodrigues
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/5271c7db-9823-411d-815f-0484e6f5536d/web|The Association of Commonwealth Universities> would like bulletins to display content in a more digestible and relevant way. The summaries after the headlines are not always very well put together, or retrieving some questionable parts of the articles. Reason behind the ask: It would be more engaging for all recipients and increase open rate.

messages.author: Will Price
messages.text: University of Cambridge has expressed frustrations about having to manually switch NLA Online on for the new searches they create. They completely appreciate the need for this workflow to ensure print volumes are managed, but as Online is more of an unlimited license, they don't understand why they need to do this for every new search. Has a fix for this been considered given it's different in to NLA print? Thanks!

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Polestar would like access to Salzburger Nachrichten print

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Coulson Partners* would like a longer archive with Signal to evidence to clients how much coverage they were getting before working with them versus post working with them.

messages.author: Charlie Gibson
messages.text: Some feedback from me (I believe others have a similar view to me) regarding the source tool. In it's current format I find it difficult to identify the basic information, such as what content set is a source available in. For example: If we use the Yorkshire Post. Looking at this page suggests that it is part of the Essentials, NewsEdge & NLA Content sets. However this is misleading because this isn't the case. In reality a customer would need NLA activated on a search to receive this source or have the NewsEdge content set. It *is not* part of essentials. I appreciate there is a box ther

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text: Mars would like journalist data in the webapp

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: Internal feedback on *AIQ Briefing* on bulletins: It would be good if we could display the "*Section Title*" as _required_ once this option is selected; The current behavior is that the bulletin section won't have a headline if this information is not added, making it confusing for clients to differentiate in which section the articles are being included (Image on this below, with the highlighted part referring to a completely different section from the Unilever one)

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/0b73a04b-0563-475c-a184-c96f6d22c0c1/web|Polestar> would like to be able to override the current sentiment for articles present in their bulletins

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/328b73c8-c43b-4765-895c-9a370651dc08/web|Zopa> would like the internal ranking for the Financial Times to be reviewed, as this is currently resulting in articles from that source being excluded from their bulletin and their preferred setup for the feature.

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text:
Sentiment feedback: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/0d9fd4af-ee91-4169-b3a8-916e50ba0100/web|Ovo Energy>
Headline: *British Gas, Scottish Power and Ovo told to 'urgently' improve customer service amid energy bill crisis*
The sentiment for the above article (syndicated across a few prominent publications) seems to be incorrectly displayed on the platform as “Positive” for the entity in question.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: Michael Page would like to be able to share foreign language content in the original language when sent via bulletin. This is because not all users in other markets speak English which makes the snippets useless to them.

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/85a01a6e-db85-4578-80b2-c9141c479074/web|Access Partnership> would like the possibility of exporting dashboards with an external email address, without necessarily having to add them as either a reader or user.

messages.author: Chloé Rodrigues
messages.text:
-- Sentiment feedback --
<https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/fd3abe78-f40a-44f6-bbc6-acff41320cb8/web|Business Growth Fund> would like mentions related to "exit" not being automatically associated to a negative sentiment. They do exit companies once their work is done with them, what means in increase of revenue for them. Sentiment on related publications tend to be automatically negative with a need to be manually corrected. It would be a nice improvement for them if context could be captured better.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Salesforce would like to filter podcasts by reach/listenership

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Sorry to raise this again, but I'd really appreciate it if we can fix the subheading issue for AIQ briefings.

It's making some of my client newsletters, such as Jera, look as though all coverage is attributed to a section that it is not relevant to.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Milltown Partners would like ability to transfer bulletins

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: A user from Tadeka has an feedback while he is in a training. When the article was mistranslated by AWS, it would be good if there is a way for users or even us (signalers) to adjust the translation

messages.author: Andre Basilio
messages.text: A user at Meta would like the ability to download the articles that are powering a graph in a Dashboard instead of the articles' CSVs from the folders that are slicing the data:

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: BCG US would like us to scrape WSJ. currently they're blocking scrapers IP addresses so there's minimal content coming through. they'd also like a running list of other sources of this nature

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Grosvenor Group* would like Custom Content to included a larger limit than 500 words to include more of the article text that they're uploading

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: A user from *Meta* would like to see an easier way to break down competitor SOV data with headline mentions.  We have visualizations to break down content by mention position but none to show comparative analysis in this fashion

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Coulson Partners: High Risk*
Referenced concerns around outages and having no proactive knowledge about significant issues. I wonder, if for future somewhat severe outages or delays we could have a small box with a warning that a client can click through to learn a little bit more about in the platform in the instance that they're concerned, so it shows an effort of us trying to be transparent with them around delays or outages. It may also help with reducing tickets to support on this regard.

My aim here is not to cause unnecessary alarm to clients who haven't realised there is an issue - b

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Takeda Japan and Germany would like to watch non-english broadcast and podcast on the platform (i.e. broadcast/podcast from other languages)

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: I'd find it really helpful if on the 'NLA On Searches' page that all of the searches that had the NLA on surfaced at the top to save me going through all the searches to figure out what is on

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
SAP use our Salience metric as a target for their local teams. They are frustrated that the Add Custom Content does not allow an option to mark an entity as High Salience/Low Salience.

They said this is causing concern and frustration across all their regional teams

(vis: <@U04GVTHBYH5>; <@U06DHQ6BDEC>; <@UF861HT2R>; <@UQD88CTQ8>)

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Brookfield Asset Management would like an alert functionality for when a organization's coverage within a specific vertical increases or decreases by 20% or more allowing them to understand if there is emerging coverage within a topic that requires their attention.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: *Disabling translation* and therefore losing access to some of the "less popular" publications is impacting our clients who compare their results from Target media in their market vs wider media (using *source location* filter) . I've seen examples of this for Polestar, CTI and more

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: SThree are trialing "topic Analysis" and woud like to be able to exclude specific reading lists and/or topics to remove the "stock and share price" noise

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: feedback from me: it'd be great if we can integrate respective CSMs into newsadmin for global deals

messages.author: Osbert Diale-Abumere
messages.text: Caroline Brogan from <https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/wvp2gmk9/companies/636a3030942c5bebd0cde2b9|Imperial College London> wants Reports to have the option of sorting clippings (added from a search result) by 'Highest Reach First' as she's not satisfied with using this filter option on a search level before clipping the search results onto a Report. <https://signal-ai.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Case/500N1000009M3QIIA0/view>

messages.author: Atiana Salazar
messages.text: My client a client wants to monitor the show “Sway in the Morning” but he’s no longer on public radio and he switched to SiriusXM…we currently don’t support this.

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text: We wont get a date for when the switch will happen

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: we've been getting lots of queries re the updated Mailgun IP. clients are asking for the confirmed date. as this is critical can we send out an intercom blast ahead of time to ensure we have a wider reach?

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: SAP would like to see less duplicate articles on the platform.  Recently they flagged 2 articles that are duplicates on the platform.  In both cases, both sources have had content merged prior to this and the content we received from our Essentials providers is the same as what we received from Lexis/NewsEdge.

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/bcab333d-7086-4a9e-bd24-d963fd8e6e2f/web|Harvine Communications> would like the possibility of filtering coverage by writers/journalists.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: Following our email to clients on automated emails, *Marsh Australia* would like to know when the IP change will take place because their “IT Team has lead times” and “If this is happening immediately I need to flag it as a critical task”.  Would we be able to share more details with the client?

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
Salesforce & Fleishman Hillard would like proximity functions such as a near operator to more relevant data.

Our lack of a near operator has been why Flieshman have recommended other tools to their clients above Signal AI

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Just walked Experian through Social360 Newsletters and their social media director said "We have been waiting our whole lives for something like this." When I said we are exploring down the line how we can bring all the data into the SaaS app: "If you have that data in the platform - it will be unlike any other tool available - every communicator will need this"

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: *Briefings* - If we set a search up to be a briefing, it looses its subheading and almost appears as though the coverage is part of whatever section there is before it. Would be good to address this for a better reader experience.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*My feedback:*
The new NLA filters makes the NLA On searches page load much slower than usual meaning you have to wait a while before you can switch the NLA on or off for a search. This wasn't taking long to load before the new release, so I wanted to flag.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from prospect Link REIT, the largest REIT in Asia: 

Problem: Names of their company, competitors and spokespeople are all wrongly translated into 4-6 different and random versions. Their PR team hopes to see non-English articles to in original languages instead if content can’t be translated correctly. To make sure we aren’t missing too many articles due to translation, we also spent a whole day or more to find out how those names / terms have been wrongly translated and put them as search terms, which is a time-demanding process.

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Waffle House would like the ability to lock a date range with a specific search so that she can compare coverage between searches without having to adjust the date as they go back and forth between the searches

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text:
Waffle House finds that Key Stories will be a huge help with identifying similar stories within the platform. However, she feels that it would not be useful unless it had the ability to:
• To show reach figures for the articles within the Key Story
• The ability to see all articles captured within that Key Story
• The ability to update sentiment in bulk for the stories captured in that Key Story

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Taylor Wessing would like access to FT's Full Disclosure email newsletter. example <https://vib-service-eu-prod.memb.ft.com/permalink/emails/eyJlbWFpbCI6ImNhNWMwYjhlN2JhMDRiZjhiNTcxZjY3MzgxNmQwMDQ4ZWRjNjU0ODUwOCIsICJ0cmFuc2FjdGlvbklkIjoiOThmNDVlNTMtZWJhNS00NWZhLTllNjMtMzEzYTMxMGEzZGMyIiwgImJhdGNoSWQiOiJjZTZjYWVlMy1lNGQzLTRkZWUtODhj|here>

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text:
Daiichi Sankyo would like the ability to further edit charts within dashboard, specifically with the "Coverage Over Time" that displays volume and reach :
• The ability to zoom in on a chart to remove whitespace that surrounds a chart during moments of spikes in coverage
• The ability to move the labeled for Coverage over time and Reach to different positions within the chart within the app
• This would allow them to edit these charts to prepare them for adding to their reports

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
The Prime Ministers & Hiscox would like to track the leaders in UK nationals. E.g. 'The Times View', 'The Telegraph View' 'The sun says' etc.

PM's office has a new lead who reportedly uses Cison/Gorkana for such monitoring. It would be super useful if there were a way to easily present such information as I think its an easy way for larger clients to be able to stay abreast of key national news in a condensed format.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Further feedback from Oxford Brookes regarding Lock functionality:
They would like if someone is in a report for it to be locked, then if someone else still wishes to go in they can break the lock with a password in cases where they need urgent access to something.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Feedback/Query:*
Can we copy a Topic Analysis from one workspace over to another persons workspace? I can't seem to do this for Uni of Arts London so wanted to check.

If we can't do this, can we please review adding this as a possible development?

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: The Wonderful Company would like to have local viewership information for the broadcast

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Praytell Agency would like to have the author information presented in the platform.

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Western Union would like Twitter/X posts to be included into bulletin alerts

messages.author: Maria Monteiro
messages.text: <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/3593f679-d071-409b-9229-2b1d7d388f72/web|Fight or Flight> would like a longer archive on the platform, as they want to conduct a historical search from 1st Jan 2022 - 31st Dec 2022; They also asked about the possibility of requesting this manually, or paying extra for this feature.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from *Fitch APAC*:
• AWS Translation has translated “Fitch”’s Chinese and Korean names sometimes into “Honour / Honor”, “Hui Hui” or “Pitch”. There are times when the names are translated correctly but the translation can be unpredictable. The problem with the wrong translations have casued them to (1) miss coverage about Fitch, (2) receive bulletins which is showing very wrong translation about the company which makes internal stakeholders question the quality of services Signal provides and question the Comms Team’s ability.
• The team have flagged this issue a few times and the cli

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: *LSEG* would like the ability to see under Insights a graph of the top orgs mentioned within a search

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback from SAP China (though we might have shared these before, they urge us to look into these again to make the platform much easier for them to use):

• They hope we can also track *WeChat* and *Broadcast channels in China, HK & Taiwan* in future
• They think too *many articles have been missed by our content providers*, and wants us to give pressure to them or find a way to improve coverage (have explained to them that Signal can do very little, but they still want us to “do something” or look for alternative providers with better scraping capabilities) 
• *Translation issues:* alth

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: ^ Lock buttons overall have been requested by multiple enterprise clients on features such as bulletins, searches, dashboards and now also reports

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text: Signal AI Reporting - Oxford Brookes. (Raising a ticket - but their Deputy head of comms is advising that the team can easily override one anothers work and would like a lock button to stop one another from being in the report when someone else already is

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Feedback from me: Timeframes*
I find I almost always have to try and select my timeframes in Signal twice before it allocating the right timeframe to my search. When I select one date, it often looks at the time frame of the start date it was already on, up to the date I selected. When i'd only just put in the first part of my query to select another part of the date. I think a couple other people in CS have mentioned this also.

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Sentiment feedback: Viatris*
1. This article was tagged as negative sentiment when it is in fact very positive.
2. I've updated it to state *positive*, but it is still showing up in the clients *negative* mentions.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: RBC would like source ranking to be updated. FT is appearing as a syndication as opposed to the lead story

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Bank of America - US would like the ability to create subfolders in their account to make it easier to navigate

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: Not sure if this is worth looking at, but a client from Archetype was looking for broadcasts for a specific product (general transcription issues) and was looking for something specific to what Cision provides in terms of data for a broadcast

messages.author: Mabel Warnock
messages.text:
*Problem:* For large Enterprise clients or prospects (e.g. KPMG or Takeda) we have exclusions searches that we want to apply across several workspaces. We want these exclusions to always match.
*Current Workflow:* The current workflow means we must manually copy a term into an exclusion search in every workspace for an organisation. This can be more than 10 workspaces. This is very manual and takes up a lot of time. Another problem happens is a user only applied an exclusion to one of the workspaces. This means that data would not match in multiple workspaces or Dashboards.
*Suggested Solution

messages.author: Kathy Tsui
messages.text: Feedback on syndicated article from *SAP*: when comes to syndicated article, they want the lead story source is the one who publish it, for example, publication A release an article, and publication B repost that article, and in the story card, show publication A as the lead source, and publication B shown in "Also appears in x sources"

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Feedback / Question from *SAP Mainland China, Hong Kong and Taiwan* :
*Problem:* They complained that Signal has been missing a number of articles which affects the accuracy of their measurement scheme  and very often our Support Team gets back to them saying the articles were “missed by content providers”.  The client wants to know how we can ensure that articles from these titles will appear on Signal again in future. Though we told them we would flag these missing articles to our content providers, they haven’t seen the volume of missing articles have gone down.

Any insight on this would b

messages.author: Paggie Leung

Feedback based on the user experience from SAP, Bain, Future Fund and other clients with a lot searches and dashboards:
*Problem:* It can take a long time (a few mins or even more) for search results or dashboards to load because of the many users or many searches or complicated feeder feeds on their workspaces. It has become very inefficient for training / demo / the clients because it can take 5x or 10x longer than it should have been to get data from the platform.
*Need:* Is it possible for us to increase the bandwidth or any work around to allow quicker system loads?
*Outcome:* Better user

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
On an SBR yesterday*, Hymans Robertson* described creating searches as *'clunky and un-intuitive'.* When digging into this I found that these are the main reasons:

1. The need for feeder feeds. When setting these up they can break searches or pull in inaccurate results due to confusion around what filter to apply to pull the right results. _(From A CS perspective, I'd agree with this. When I start applying feeder feeds I really need to think what I'm doing to get the correct results. In theory we position ourselves as self serve but when it comes to more complex searches, it isn’t the most in

The whole word had to be spelled out in order for it to surface, which can also annoy some clients who may not know to write out the source as 1 word instead of 2

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
*Dashboard charts - Permira.*
All of our dashboard insights use an anchoring search, but sometimes, that anchoring search, while an important feeder, isn't always appropriate in some of the analysis our clients want to do.E.g. Permira want a pie chart to look at their own coverage break down, looking only at Permira mentions within the 6 themes.

In Topic analysis, we can move a data point from the charts if it isn't relevant and save it. If there was a way to remove a data point just from a specific chart within the workspace, that would be useful to tailor insights further

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Just a few more bits of feedback about search level custom filters:

1. When adding a chart to a search within insights you  have to save a chart twice, then delete the second before being able to add your next chart. seems like a bug that would be good to clear.
2. When adding a custom filter to one search, it applies to all searches (as you can see in the image in the feedback comment above. In many ways this is very helpful for efficiencies. On the other hand it would be good if I could delete filters that don't make sense for the specific search in question, without it removing from all ot

messages.text:
When you add a new filter into a search, I have to refresh the whole page in order for the new filter to show. It doesn't seem to automatically populate.

The Workflow that has been built for the Prime Minister's office will likely require the team to be in and out of these filters frequently, so having to refresh in order for the new filter to show isn't a great UX.

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
Topic Analysis Feedback from BCG

BCG believe the following additions would make the Topic Analysis feature impactful:
• The ability to add exclusions and exclusion feeders when building the analysis to reduce noise and incorrect mentions
• The ability to group a set of topics together or select an existing folder of topics for an analysis. This would allow them to have a preset view for topics of interest to them eg Deep Tech topics
• On the two quadrant graphs, they would like us to add in a line/marker to show the focus organization average association and sentiment so they can see which to

messages.author: Fabian Cornwell
messages.text:
Also to add to Sergey’s good news, we’ve removed Journalists :sleuth_or_spy: from the Search bar :mag:!

Please note that any searches that already include or exclude journalists we will leave the setup in it’s current form. So we will not be breaking any clients searches.

If anybody wants this turned back on, please contact myself as we have the Journalist search filter feature flagged. If we don’t hear any news in the next few months we will be deleting this permanently from the Web App.

messages.author: Sergey Maximov

For your convenience and timesaving we added the option to deactivate all actively scheduled bulletins for organisation to News admin.
To access it, go to organisation -> web platform -> scroll all the way down to Active Scheduled Bulletins section.

Please be warned, that this is very blunt tool, it disables all active bulletins in the organisation and if re-activation is necessary, you would need to do it manually.

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
*IFA* would be interested in Topic Modeling or Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis.

These seem like they could add a layer of depth to our analysis, especially for complex topics like 'food security'. These approaches, from what I gather, offer a more detailed type of sentiment analysis. They not only determine the overall sentiment (like positive, negative, or neutral) of a text, but also connect it to specific details or features within a wider topic.

For example:- connect 'food security' to various dimensions like affordability, availability, and nutritional value

For context, those are:
• Each time an organisation name appears in the headline = *1.0*
• Each time an organisation name appears in the summary (first few lines of the article) = *0.2*
• Each time an organisation name appears inside a quote = *0.2*
• Each time an organisation name appears in the body of the article = *0.05*

Just a suggestion from with regard to the latest NLA update.
First - thank you for deploying so quickly off the back of the feedback shared. Whilst, I love that this will stop clients mindlessly brining in a lot of irrelevant NLA coverage and running up unnecessary charges, the update does somewhat effect usability /user friendliness, so I have a suggestion to share.

1. For the wording, start it with "To protect our clients..." so they can have the tone it is an update not burden them with more manual work, but to protect their usage...
2. Add 'click here' to find out why... at the end of the

Thanks!

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need*: Salesforce want us to address our Salience weighted scoring since they believe the current scoring to be prohibitive. Alternatively, they would like to be able to filter articles within the web app based on the number of mentions of an entity within the article.

*Problem*: SFDC do not believe that our Salience scoring is an accurate reflection of the relevance of an article since the current scoring methodology only attributes 0.05 to a mention of an entity in the body of the article. This means that an entity would need to be mentioned 20 times in the body of an article to be deemed

messages.author: U02DBGKTDUN
messages.text:
*Duplication - RVC - Data Quality & Efficiency*

I've raised this with individuals across content/product but wanted to log for broader awareness. One big Pain point for RVC is duplicates. I raised this on this channel recently, but following a discovery call with Nick, the root cause of this issue appears to be more transparent.

Due to the way we ingest the name of different sources from different providers, we are often logging two of the exact same article, with the exact same weblink as two separate articles (<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bYZMZSoI3XpXyjBHQOiOgRT5-2AL1tt1kXX

We have gone through the historical feedback and identified a backlog of challenging user “intents” we can’t easily addre

DAZN (£70k ARR; at high risk of churn in Q3)
Need: The ability to save searches and the time frame you are observing them within that saved search

messages.author: Carl Lajoie

Need: The ability to compare searches to each other but within selected time frame. For example, comparing Search A's coverage for the month of January to Search B's coverage for the month of February

Outcome: Easier and objective campaign comparison

*Outcome:* Allows the CS team to more efficiently audit client's bulletins and reports.

*Problem* - Salesforce have created complex dashboard with multiple filter based upon each user’s requirements. In order for each user to get the article level data based on each applicable filter, they would need to create thousands of searches.

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need* - Boston Consulting Group want our Dashboards to include the option of adding Salience graphs, similar to our sentiment graphs. Additionally, they want it to be a soft filter.
*Problem* - Dashboards don’t include the same soft filters that they can use in the Insights page at search/folder level. Additionally, they are unable to view the salient articles overtime.
*Outcome* - Allowing our clients to filter by a critical metric in our dashboards.

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need* - Boston Consulting Group want our Dashboards to exclude Twitter engagement from the graphs. They do not believe it adds value to the visual and would like a toggle to remove it at a Dashboard level. They also do not consider our Reach data to add value to the visuals.
*Problem* - BCG think the Twitter Engagement overlay on the graphs are a distraction which takes away from the value of the visual.
*Outcome* - By offering a toggle to turn off the Twitter engagement data at a Dashboard level, it could increase this client’s usage of our Dashboards and avoid their team spending time expla

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need* - Boston Consulting Group want our Dashboards to include the sentiment at a competitor analysis level.
*Problem* - They dislike the idea of creating a dashboard per competitor to access the sentiment analysis and believe comparing it within our system will be difficult due to the information living in different Dashboards.
*Outcome* - A more usable Dashboard with greater insights on competitor sentiment analysis. It will also allow for a cleaner and more accessible Dashboard.

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need* - Boston Consulting Group want our Dashboards to include a graph which includes all topics, not just the topics driving the Dashboard.
*Problem* - They find it cumbersome to go between the search/folder level results to find information which could live in the Dashboard. They believe it will be a training issue as more of their company onboard to the platform in that users will look at a Dashboard and believe the present topics are the only topics being talked about
*Outcome* - By adding a graph to look at all topics being discussed it will help our clients see the unknown unknowns with

Hey CS :wave: the web platform team are currently looking at *improving the look and feel of email bulletins to be more compact and easier to digest information.*

Feedback from me and many clients:
*NEED*: To get the NLA Online usage/estimation on the platform and control it.
*PROBLEM*: The NLA is increasingly pushing a volume restrictive NLA online licenses, they use the usage stats they get from us to structure the renewal usage and cost. Client cannot currently see any of this information meaning they don’t know how to proceed and how to potentially be in control of those figures (We don’t even have an understanding internally what the usage number includes!) Also - some clients are also choosing the NLA coverage for specific titles only - which we c

messages.author: U047L4HECGN
messages.text:
hello CS folks

Right now in a Saved Search, the header only shows the given title of the search, and you can only see the actual query/search terms by clicking on the edit button and open the search edit panel.

I have been having a hunch that this might be a bit of a bad UX, as you can't easily glance and get a sense of the search context; <@U02C74071R7> today also flagged this issue.

Did we get any feedback around this piece of UI?

He only had his Mobile and had to call a colleague to get this set up on a desktop. All he'd like is the ability do the quick tasks such as search/bulletin creation in an efficient prompt manner to respond to such crisis when hes on the

messages.author: UTCKKCC6L
messages.text:
Hi there, we also made some improvement to the search bar in the App.
Now it should be easier to find entities with special characters in their name. We also made it a bit more forgiving for typos, when no results found for an original query, we try to find closest word to it.

If you remember of any examples of some entity not returning, can you please try it now and let us know if it is still happening. Other feedback regarding search of entities is welcomed too!

messages.author: Fabian Cornwell
messages.text:
<!here> We’ve just launched our new beta ‘Key Stories’ to the Signal org!

This new feature is powered by the Events API capability and helps you understand the key storylines and narratives around a subject of interest (entity, topic or phrase).

Please have a look and if you have any questions or find any bugs message me or on the <#C025U47TBFX> channel. Thanks!

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need*: Boston Consulting Group want to be able to customize the colours in our dashboard graphs, in particular, when looking at their competitors. I know this is a common request!
*Problem:* The lack of customized colours can lead to competitors being listed with the wrong colours or the colours of a different company listed on the same graph which can cause confusion. This makes our graphs unusable in their current form and require manipulation prior to being shared more widely in our clients’ orgs.
*Outcome*: By changing the colours to their desired palette, they will be able to create dash

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need*: Boston Consulting Group want sentiment per competitor in the one dashboard in order to track each companies’ sentiment over time and across different topics.
*Problem:* The present setup doesn’t allow you to view competitors’ sentiment in the same dashboard. The requires making additional dashboards to capture this information which leads to busy workspaces and dashboards that exist with very limited value beyond a couple of graphs.
*Outcome*: Better market insight in a more efficient and accessible way.

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need*: Boston Consulting Group want a filter or dropdown menu at the top of dashboards so they could toggle between multiple organizations. This would require allowing multiple anchor organizations to a dashboard but it would enable our client to populate the same dashboard layout while looking through the lens of their organization and then switching to their competitor’s organization. We have a similar dropdown menu in the Affinity Beta in the Web App.
*Problem:* The current setup requires multiple Dashboards to capture the data per organization. *Outcome*: This would alleviate the problem

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need*: When looking at the story card and csv download, Boston Consulting Group would like to know the what the leading topic is for each article. Alternatively, they would be happy if we ordered the topics based on the relevance of the topic to the entity. Could either of these be done by integrating Affinity into the story card?
*Problem*: Articles are tagged with so many topics that it can be difficult for clients to understand the true “aboutness” of the article without reading it.
*Outcome*: It would allow clients to surface the most relevant articles relating to each topic. This would h

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need:* WE Communications Affinity - They would like to be able to add filters (similar to dashboard filters) for the Affinity output in the Web App, in particular, reading lists, sentiment, salience. They want the topic filter where selecting a topic would reveal sub-topics and related topics on the graph. They would also like to be able to include exclusions to reduce noise.

*Problem*: The current view is too broad which causes the highlighted topics to be the broader topics. It doesn’t allow for insights into the sub-topics and related topics driving the larger topic. Additionally, this ca

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need* - WE Communications Affinity: When creating an Affinity view, they would like to be able to base it off a folder level search which includes relevant topics and exclusions.

*Problem*: The current setup is a catch all for every topic and does not allow clients to see more nuanced topics easily. If they could build the Affinity search upon folders and exclusions, they would then like us to populate sub-topics and related topics.

*Outcome*: Their request would allow for a more refined, customizable tool with richer insights. It would also alleviate data quality issues.

messages.author: U1NR8QYKB

The article from Bloomberg was added as a custom story:

*Need:*  WE Communications want article level data regarding the Headline/Lead Paragraph/Quote metric to be included in the csv download.

*Outcome*: Providing this metric would allow our client to provide greater context and deeper insights to their end clients. This would further increase the depth of our stickiness with the

*Viatris* - AIQ Briefing picks top 10 articles for each subsection.
Teresa would like a toggle option to make this a max of 5 articles per subsection, for example, so that the team are not seeing _quite_ so much coverage in the daily alert and can jump into the platform for broader deeper info.

messages.author: Conor Gorman
messages.text:
*Need*: WE Communications require a unique ID for each article in the downloaded csv. They have noticed that the “Story ID” is not unique. They are able to get a unique document ID from the API but not the Web App. They mentioned that “currently the url changes with each export”.
*Problem*: WE Comms are download multiple csv files from different parts of the Web App and merge them into a database. Without having unique IDs per article, they are unable to merge their data sets.
*Outcome*: By having a unique document ID, we allow our clients to blend our data into one database for additional ins

*Need* - Mastercard wants to choose 2 sentiments in the dashboard (i.e. filter part)
*Problem* - Client can only choose positive sentiment or neutral sentiment, but not positive + neutral

Who? Whataburger :whataburger:

messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Need*: Boston Consulting Group want more accurate numbers on reach and are asking if we have worked with SimilarWeb on their additional capabilities to narrow the reach number to site section, and in some case specific articles.
*Problem*: Our reach numbers often look inflated due to the current calculation method used by SimilarWeb. This makes this metric unusable to certain clients.
*Outcome*: By working with SimilarWeb we could create a method to receive more accurate and useful reach numbers.

messages.author: Charlotte Dialdas
messages.text:
*Need* - The IET would like to have the option to choose if syndicates are also tagged when tagging a lead story
*Problem* - They manually tag articles by quality and the tag varies based on a number of factors including source, prominence etc. Currently, syndicated articles are being tagged with the incorrect quality score, because they are tagged alongside the lead story
*Outcome* - More flexible use of current feature and more accurate reporting

Pega have also flagged the same issue

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
*Need* - FIS APAC notices that the search bar for adding media to reading lists is not as sensitive as it used to be. Previously, she didn’t need to type the full name / exact name / most of the name of the media to be able to select it, but now she needs to.
*Problem* - How we name media titles on Signal maybe different from how our clients call them. This is especially challenging for media in newer Signal markets e.g. India, China or APAC as the media might have not been used or checked by the support team as much as the other media in more developed markets.
*Outcome* - Clients can build r

*Need*: WE Communications need the source article which broke a story first clearly marked in the csv download. The need this “Parent” flag and the ID to connect the syndicated stories back to the original “Parent”
*Problem*: The current “Story Lead?” column in the csv download does not indicate the original source since it is based on the source importance, and it is ranked by our content provider based on popular

messages.author: U032YC3S29H
messages.text:
Request from me:

If a client has a limited number of Briefings in their package, the platform should not enable them to select more briefings than they are entitled to.

Currently, the customer can select an unlimited number of briefings and simply receive a warning, whereas practically they shouldn't be able to add more briefings than their contract entitles them to without asking their CS rep.

In my current situation, I have a client who i'm due to onboard who has 1 Briefing in their contract but they've turned it on for 5 of their searches.

Though they are aware that we have Twitter content, they need to find tools to cover areas we do not cover in the next few months - which may pose threats to the nex

messages.author: U03SGKR9ZS4

What: Would like it if Bulletins & Reports could display a headline or preview or an article even for recipients that aren't a reader or don't have NLA access.
Why: Not all of their recipients will need to actually read an article but it's beneficial for their media roundup if people can get a top line view of what's going on

messages.text: WHAT / WHO: Novartis would like to have a historic archive of data, WHY: so they can run YoY comparison reports

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text:
It would be great if we could have *several folders available to be used as the “Target media”* on the Dashboard.

This would then allow client to flip between “Target media Tier 1" / “Target media Tier 2” / “all media” rather than categorising everything in one folder and clicking individually when there is a further media type breakdown like in *Michael’s Page* example:

messages.text: it'd be great if we can allow special characters and hyphens in the typeahead without needing concept ops intervention

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text:
Can we please look again at adding *proximity* of a *keyword/topic mention in relation to the entity* (even if it is only relating to the organisation)? This is how we apply our sentiment and I heard Meltwater is offering this solution.
At the moment looking at “sustainability” topic + Amazon organisation (as an example) still often brings up coverage which includes both of these, but in no relation to one another. This means insights on the dashboard are not always accurate.. This would be very powerful feature for our *strategic external knowledge provider* positioning.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
Can we add an toggle option to bulletins for any time cadence other than 'real time' that would still send through a bulletin even if there is no coverage and say 'No Relevant Coverage'?

There has been some confusion lately with a few clients lately thinking that bulletins aren't working, when really there just hasn't been coverage recently. This way a client can be informed either way if desired

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

messages.author: Thomas Carey

*Solution -* Remove duplicated articles / mention which stories were included in other searches so that Bulletins are no longer repeating the same story.

In this case, could the below message be changed when deactivating a User via News Admin? It is quite misleading and creates a lot of confusion around this topic.

*Feedback from FleishmanHillard*: They prefer use our Boolean competitors for topic searches since his colleagues are often unsure of exactly what our topics cover. messages.author: U0449M1SGTB
messages.text:
*Feedback from FleishmanHillard:* Miles said the reason some of their teams prefer to use our Boolean competitors is the ability to use a “Near Operator” search function. Without this function in our system, they feel that our results pull in a lot of irrelevant coverage which takes manual work to clean.

messages.text:
*Feedback From Signal, Commetric & Clients - Regarding: Salience Score*

Is there a possibility to adjust the _"*Salience*"_ ranges?

At the moment _*High Salience*_ is really similar to Headline mention, while _*low salience*_ cuts a lot of quality coverage for our clients.

_*As an Example*_ - A typical corporate press release that does not have an headline mention will fall within the Salience range of 0.5 and 1.0 - with our current methodology this article will have a _*Low Salience*_ score, however, this shouldn't be the case.

Is this date range something Signal would be willing to reconsider?”

*Need* - Amnesty International (apologies they have a lot of feedback!) would also like to be able to remove the number of articles in the title of a news bulletin (ie (51 stories in The Guardian + 33 more publications)).
*Problem* - They find the information mis-leading as this headline in particular insinuates that they have 51 Guardian stories which isn't the case - its 51 stories in 34 publications.
*Outcome* - This would remove confusion and mis-leading bulletins

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
Amnesty International would like to be able to receive a weekly news bulletin with just stats in it for the *year to date.* 

They would like these stats to include information such as:

• Breakdown of print, online, broadcast mentions since Jan 1st
• Number of tier 1 titles since Jan 1st
• Number of high salient articles since Jan 1st
• Number of mentions in relation to their key messaging since Jan 1st
I mentioned that all this data is available within the platform but they say a 'round up of stats' would be useful to receive automatically to further help track their performance.

They'd als

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text:
For Columbia Threadneedle from Marble, me and support team:
*Need*: easily update and identify top mentioned investment funds for the analysis
*Problem*: client has over 200+ existing funds which also often change the names. These have to be divided into several folders as the page doesn’t load and expires (even when there is very little coverage!). It also requires manual (long and prone to error) updates of the names.
*Outcome*: easier bulk data download from a single folder. Possibility of upload excel keywords to update/ create new searches

Also from Amnesty:

*Need* - Option to reformat bulletins.
*Problem* - Currently bulletins are quite long and Kai would like to make the text/layout tighter. She wants the option to resize headings and articles.
*Outcome* - the layout of bulletins would be cleaner and easier to digest

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
Feedback from Amnesty International:

*Need* - They'd like to have the option to remove grouping articles within email bulletins
*Problem* - Currently some sources come through under 'also appears in'  or 'similar articles' and some stories 'get lost' if they are in either of these sub-sections
*Outcome* - Client would be able to see each individual article without having to click through to separate links within their bulletin

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text: It would be great if users were prompted with a warning _before_ deleting a Dashboard. The current workflow is that, after deleting a Dashboard, the client is prompted with an "Undo" button that appears at the bottom of the screen. This is a bit confusing for clients, as we've now had multiple clients accidentally delete a Dashboard accidentally without realizing they can undo it. A warning such as "Are you sure you want to delete your dashboard? All of your information will be lost" should deter clients from accidentally deleting their dashboards.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: Have we thought about creating a Signal AI 'Impact Score' using all of the metadata we  have? (Sentiment, Saliency, Proximity...etc)? I think that could be a really powerful feature to roll out, as we could even allow clients to add in specific criteria such as their Tier 1 media lists and key topics/key messages to track against the score. They could then track their impact scores overtime in the dashboard, and even against competitors

messages.author: Lu Zhang
messages.text:
Hi all,
It’s been great to see so many of you try to give more context when providing user feedback, which is really helpful for the product team to diagnose the problem and offer a right solution.

I also noticed the current format especially the NEED and the OUTCOME can not always be separated easily. If you struggle with it, the following questions might help you to dig deeper of the real issue from your clients:

• What are our users doing today that they couldn’t do with our system? *[PROBLEM]*
• In the future what would be the thing that the “requested feature” would allow them to do? *[

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text:
Feedback from me - when going to “*Reading lists*” => “*Organisation Lists*”:
1. Can that *“Organisation Lists”* Tab be the first one available as default? I don’t really know a client who uses the other 2 tabs (Signal lists/ All Lists).
2. If not, can we at least stop the automatic switch to the “All Lists” tab once we click into a specific Reading List?
It is rather confusing and requires you to then go back to the “Organisation lists” tab if you want to switch to the next Reading list.

*Outcome* - showcasing results and gaining better engagement from w wider audience within the business, potentially further upsell opportunities - reader seats/ users etc

(Feedback from me - though this should have been flagged before)

*Need* - The ability to see if a user logs in via a different IP address or from another location.  And ideally locks one’s account if he / she is logging in from multiple countries in a short period of time.
*Problem* - To avoid seat sharing, which hurts our revenue. Currently there is no way for Success to find out if a user shared his / her login details with others, and a lot of them should be doing this.
*Outcome* - Allows fair usage and protects revenue.

*Outcome* - More user friendly and more efficient for the client.

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T
messages.text:
*High Salience:*
Query -
As I understand it, _*High Salience*_ mentions of an entity mean the organisation mention will need to either appear in the Headline of an article or at least 4 mentions in the body of the article

Suggestion-
_*High Salience*_ could include mentions in the *Headline / Lead Paragraph* or 4 mentions in the body of the article

Why-
I believe it makes more sense as it covers off instantly all those articles where the entity is mentioned in the headline or lead paragraph (leading us to believe the content is likely to be 100% relevant for a company), but also accounts for

messages.text: Tradeweb would like the ability to tailor competitor's color on SOV chart. The customer would like to keep the associated colors pertaining to each client constant for consistent reporting functions.

messages.author: U032YC3S29H
messages.text:
:arrow_up: Connected to the above :arrow_up:

*Need:* The client would like to have the option to add all the coverage within their report without having to add them individually.

*Problem:* When curating coverage reports within Signal, the client has to add all the individual sources as separate stories, even if they are simply duplicate coverage.

*Outcome:* A way to group all similar/same coverage (syndicates) on a report in the same way that it appears in saved searches & story cards.

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
*Need* - The ability to see coverage reach on syndications (duplicate coverage in different sources)

*Problem -* Harpswood Agency currently have to find each specific source the coverage comes through from in order to provide the coverage reach data to the client. At the moment if coverage appears in multiple sources, the i button only shows the coverage for 1 source, not all of them.

*Outcome -* Add an i button next to all the sources (where it says 7h ago next to Oxford Mail for example) so that they can quickly see the coverage reach for all of the publications where the coverage appears.

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text:
*Need*: Clients need to be able to order the following chart by descending order.
*Problem:* The following chart is displaying data improperly. For example, this is displaying two competitors with no data while the second page of the chart has data that should be displayed.
*Outcome:* Clients can see the data in descending order, providing a much more appealing visual chart.

messages.author: UN05S2C7Q

messages.author: U02QDBK7V39

*Outcome*: be able to get counts on product concepts mention

*Problem:* GIC was looking for an article in “Yahoo! Singapore” but the source cannot be found as the exclamation mark (!) cannot be recognised. We tried to find this search by typing Yahoo, or Singapore, which gives a lot of other sources instead. We have “Yahoo Singapore”, “Yahoo SingaporeFinance” but those sources do not have the article the client looks for.
*Outcome:* (1) Easier for clients to look fo

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
*Need:* Improvement on translation
*Problem:* Shangri-La Hong Kong missed two-third or more of the coverage on a recent campaign due to translation issues. Instead of translating the Chinese term “藝術＠維港” into “Art@Harbour”, we have the half-translated term “Art ＠維港” on the platform. Another term has been translated into “Broken Hong Kong”, and it was supposed to be “Blossoming Hong Kong” (綻放香港). Due to these misses, they could find more results on Google than on Signal and has been upset about this. We tried to help fix the issue by adding “”Art ＠維港” as a search term, which allowed more result

messages.text: Olive AI would like to have more capability on training the entities within the platform. While they have been using the "Does this mention the entity" feature on the storycards. They would like to be able to influence the platform more so that they can help train the AI

Hound Agency exports Signal’s data into Ace Media - where they put together this kind of dashboards for their clients’ reports.

*Mark Barbaree* from *Syneos Health* would like the ability to limit search results by:

2. the term's appearance within the headline or first paragraph

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text: Mark Barbaree from Syneos Health would like search terms to be included in Microsoft Excel exports.

messages.author: U06URNEH3

Feedback from me:

Homepage gets messy when you have "Topic" searched that bring up 100K+ articles a day (those topics searches power dashboards and are not paired with Entities)

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text:
Request from me - It would be beneficial if Bulletins would have the name of the Workspace they are sent from included in the e-mail. At present, recipients reach out to Support in order to be removed from Bulletins, and there is a lot of extra work in checking which workspace they're being sent from when checking the Mailgun logs.

Another quick win would be giving recipients the option to unsubscribe from Bulletins directly from the e-mail instead of reaching out to Support. We receive a lot of these requests regularly and a simple email removal requires a lot of investigation on our part.

messages.author: U032YC3S29H
messages.text:
*Need*: Ability to embed images into reports and customise them

*Problem*: Our report functionality doesn't allow customers to easily embed charts / images into the body of the report they send out. It only allows for them to either add as attachment or download as word doc and try to format it to include images at certain points (not the easiest thing to do). This leads them to require using 3rd party tools like Mailchimp to do their reports

*Outcome:* Higher usage of reports and provides more control to the clients to customise their reports before they send them out

Rather that the 24 hour view that we offer, the WorldBank want to select the time range to the hour.
Eg 9am to 9am the next day (we currently offer midnight to midnight.)

Advised her to download each quarter going forward, but raising for awareness.

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
*Need:* Shangri-La needs the ability to _detect punctuation marks / special characters in the search bar._ 
*Problem:* Although we have trained 100+ Shangri-La related entities for them, they don’t come up if they type Shangri-La in the search bar as the “hyphen” won’t be recognised. For example, they will need to type “Singapore”, if they want the “Shangri-La Singapore” entity to show up. However, if there are / will be many entities with the word Singapore in it, it would be difficult for them to find it.
Also, a lot of clients don’t know special characters can’t be recognised, hence they ma

messages.author: Mabel Warnock
messages.text:
*Need:* The ability to sort articles by Reach in searches
*Problem:* At the moment clients can only sort by Relevancy or by time. If they want to see any prioritisation of Reach they must manually create a reading list, duplicate searches and use the comparison feature.
*Outcome:* Prioritising by reach would enable clients to discover where their key mentions are happening. This would help to increase Signal's "stickiness" for PR&Coms

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
