messages.author: U039X8NR17X
messages.text: Aggie Dent from DAZN would like to see searches ranked in order of relevancy, saliency, or reach

messages.author: Thomas Carey
messages.text:
*Need:* The ability to set a default between 'Target Media' & 'All Media' on Dashboards
*Problem:* Although iwoca have built several reading lists for their key media, however, they have to change the drop down menu to all media every time so that they can make sure they aren't missing over 50% of their coverage coming through.
*Outcome:* Provide clients the option to set which media (target or all) is loaded onto Dashboards on the set-up page. Enabling them to quickly set-up their preferences and limit the amount of repetitive tasks they would have to do each time they went to the dashboa

messages.author: Carl Lajoie
messages.text: Alex Judd of Clarity does not like the user experience behind dashboard. Specifically the high amount of searches he has to create for his clients in order to see coverage for them and be able to export the necessary data from those dashboard for reports

messages.author: UF8P05XT6
messages.text:
*Need*: Shangri-La would like a less complicated process for building multiple dashboards on the platform
*Problem:* Having to duplicate searches on the platform to add into the dashboard is confusing and makes the folders on the platform very cluttered
*Outcome:* Simply the process and make it more apparent how to quickly build dashboards. The team mentioned that it is very easy and obvious how to build dashboards on Meltwater through a series of logical buttons on their platform.

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9

messages.text:

*Need* - Bain would like to have the ability to add sub-tags or have a more sophisticated tagging system (e.g. interlinking tags)
*Problem* - An article could have mentioned Bain and also their competitors but touched on different sub categories or have different sentiment towards different entities in the same piece which they want to tag separately.
*Outcome* - It would be easier for them to pull data correctly based on tags.

*Problem* - Currently the contents section only includes the number of Lead articles included within a search that powers bulletins. Whilst they understand that we can't include each and every article within a big search in bulletins, they believe the number in the content page should equal the total number in the search powering the bulletin. This discrepancy means that they can't send through bulletins to senior stakeholders and in

3. The ability to track Foreign Language articles. E.g. Signal helped detect a negative Russia

*Problem:* For example, they want to find out articles about LGBTQ+ in Thailand media are mainly positive / neutral / negative.

messages.author: Lucas Martino
messages.text:
*Internal need:* We need a way to split Global accounts in NewsAdmin.
Shangri-La, for example, have 15 users and it’s impossible to easily spot who’s EMEA, APAC or US.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
*User need:* Bain’s Shelly would like the ability to filter news by reach
*Problem:* Clients do not always have a media list, and they want to get news from media outlets with higher reach. Currently, they have to download all the search results, try to find out which media has the lowest reach, and ask support to create a search to exclude all those media.
*Outcome:* Giving clients more flexibility in getting relevant / targeted results, and saving the client / the Product Ops Team’s time in creating an exclusion search.

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8

*Problem* - In shared workspaces I have a client who is wanting to separate their individual dashboards _without_ the need to simply put their name in the title of the dashboard. Currently unable to custom arrange the order due to the fact that they are ordered alphabetically.
*Outcome -* Enable users to drag and drop dashboards in the same way you can with saved searches

On top of this, the client mention is rarely isolated in the small snippet received within the broadcast section of the bulletin (unlike with online/print coverage), so, it's difficult for the client to understand the context of the broadcast piece. It takes time for them to log into the platform and listen back to each of their broadcast clips to determine the rele

• A way to know who added a tag to a story (on shared workspace), as well as the ability to pull out articles that are tagged by a particular person
• Autopopulating and interlinking some tags
*Problem: “*It is very confusing to have to include every syndicated art

This is a problem for our agency clients who rely on our graphical options for reporting.

*Outcome:* Alex has asked me to feed this back with a contextual example. This is that, if a specific key word was to consi

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

_Feedback from me_
*NEED:* setting up user who is a current email recipient or reader on another account (for example bulletin from their PR agency who is also Signal client)
*PROBLEM:* currently we cannot use the same user to set up as platform user and email recipient/reader
*OUTCOME:* overcomplicated and inefficient process of deletion/ email alteration required (<mailto:xx+1@xxx.com|xx+1@xxx.com>)

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: Also, now if you remove a folder from the dashboard build the widgets still remain on the dashboard unless you go through and physically delete them. Can this be automated?

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
Also, now when you add a new folder to a dashboard, it doesn't automatically add in widgets. For example, if I add in a topic folder, nothing changes in the dashboard unless I go into the chart library and select the new visualizations I want.

To that end, if I remove or update a folder that is currently in the Dashboard - it change isn't fully reflected unless I delete the old widget and add the new one back in.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: Additionally, for spokespeople - the update made it so that searches with data no longer surface at the top. You now need to click through to see which spokespeople had mentions. Can we change this back so that anyone with data appears first? I am concerned clients will just assume there is no data and mis-report

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: With the new dashboard update today, clicking the 'X' no longer exits the popup. Currently you need to click outside the pop up itself. Can this be updated?

messages.author: U01K0C0HUCT
messages.text:
Second item from Michael Osborne at Clifford Chance:

*Need* - Ability to customize / re-order Insights widgets to suit his preferences of what he wants to see when clicking “insights” (not sure if this needs to be persisted at a search level, user level or workspace level.)
*Problem* - There are some widgets that he really cares about and references a lot on Insights, and some he doesn’t need at all. It would make his work a bit easier if he was able to just have the insights widgets that he likes when clicking the insights tab.
*Outcome* - Users able to priortize the things they care about i

messages.author: U01K0C0HUCT
messages.text:
Met Michael Osborne from Clifford Chance at the Exec Connect breakfast and he mentioned a QoL feature and something a bit bigger. Starting with the QoL:

*Need* - When viewing dashboard or insights widgets, having the ability to “Copy to Clipboard” the image/png.
*Problem* - Michael often likes to include some of our images in slides or emails, but doesn’t like how he has to either (download and then find-and-insert them) or (take a screenshot and paste). It interrupts his workflow (and means we don’t get that interaction data in the screenshot approach).
*Outcome* - Users more easily able to

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text: With the release of Bloomberg direct content, it would be great if this was prioritized over Moreover/Opoint scrapes. A client flagged to us an abundance of coverage that came in with irregular content from an incorrect Moreover scrape, but this shouldn't be the case since we got proper content directly from Bloomberg.

messages.author: Charlotte Dialdas
messages.text:
Reporting feature feedback from Baker McKenzie:

*Need* - Article headlines to be editable
*Problem* - Rarely some headlines include symbols (in place of particular letters) which can be frustrating and look messy
*Outcome* - Neater reports

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
Reporting feature feedback from Baker McKenzie:

*Need* - Within reports, it would be good if comments attached to an article, rather than stand alone.
*Problem* - The team spend a lot of time putting reports together and make comments on particular articles. They add a lot of articles to this report and then need to re-arrange them to put in order of importance. However, when articles are moved, the comments don't move with them which is frustrating.
*Outcome* - It will save clients time and reduce errors in reporting

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Solomon Partners would like Roxhill's Database integrated into Signal AI so they can just use one platform as opposed to both

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
*User Need* - More global / APAC clients (e.g. Mastercard, Mercer, Heidrick & Struggles, Barclays, Pegasystems, Dropbox, Citibank, Square) are asking for content from Nikkei publications in Japan
*User Problem -* Nikkei, which is the biggest and a reputable financial and national publisher owning a number of national Nikkei titles and trade publications, is an important media brand for many local, regional and global clients. With a lot of global deals coming on board with analysis reports and measurement programmes, clients think the reports are less convincing and not accurate when we do

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
*User Need* - Olam wants to see bylines in LexisNexis articles
*User Problem -* The client wants to know who has written an article - and such info is normally available in the newspaper / website but LexisNexis does not include it.
*Outcome -* Clients can better monitor crisis or maintain relationship with journalists based on what the journalists have written about them.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
Can we update the 'article' figure in the dashboards/searches to be *accurate* rather than an estimation when referencing large qualities of articles? In order for dashboards to be useful, the clients need to see accurate data there.

Clients are flagging that the breakdowns, such as sentiment, don't add up to the total number. This is opening up our platform to huge scrutiny and the clients are questioning our data.

messages.author: Fabian Cornwell
messages.text:
<!here> Ahead of the launch of the new dashboards functionality on Monday April 11th. We have now enabled the new functionality for all dashboards in the Signal organisation, please have a play around with the new feature in your own accounts and inform us if you find any bugs.

Thanks!

messages.author: Charlotte Dialdas
messages.text:
We've had this one before but:

*Need* - Leigh Day would like to be able to create sub-folders
*Problem* - Currently, client workspaces are getting  messy with multiple users being able to create different searches within shared workspaces. Clients spend a lot of time searching for the searches/folders that they need without any particular order.
*Outcome* - Whilst the introduction of being able to search for a folder has been super useful, the ability to filter by sub-folders will save time and increase efficiency for lots of clients

messages.author: U02R13135BK
messages.text:
<https://signalhq.slack.com/archives/CKZELQY5T/p1647944844620269>
I have had some further feedback from the client:

• It is about the Report section
• They would like the custom stories to be attached to the 'Headers', so it is easier to move them around as a group.
    ◦ They often attach multiple stories to their headers and may want to change the sorting priority
        ▪︎ To get around this, they have to move each tab one by one
• They would also like for Headers which include no stories to not be published on the final report
    ◦ But to also keep the Headers active, for ease of editin

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text:
*User Need*: For all clients - being able to track how many reader seats/workspace/ active searches  etc are used/remaining (to indicate for example 3 workspaces out of 4 are being used)
*User Problem:* At the moment Account Management section shows "used' number of reader seats/active searches etc, but it doesn't indicate how many are included in the contract
*Outcome*: No visibility if they've gone over the limit

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text:
*User Need*: Hiscox would like to compare their sentiment to that of their competitors.
*User Problem:* Currently, they would have to build a separate dashboard for al of the competitors to be able to see and use the filter to view the results for each of the companies.
*Outcome*: No need for several dashboards, more user friendly comparison.

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text:
*User Need*: World Bank would like the ability to compare folders.
*User Problem:* Currently, they would have to build individual searches with the searches from those folders as feeders in order to compare folder results.
*Outcome*: Better comparative tools for search results.

messages.author: Anita Wang
messages.text:
*User Need*: Sam Brown would like the ability to download interactive dashboard graphs for their customers.
*User Problem*: Current download options does not provide visual interactive points for the selected chart/graphs
*Outcome*: Ease of sharing data and enhances visual digestion of graphs

messages.author: U02R13135BK
messages.text:
*User Need*: _London Stock Exchange_ - Coverage on the Dashboard to not be limited to 70 searches
*User Problem*: They have over 70 searches for different spokespeople they want to track mentions/quotes. As the current limit is 70, some of this data is not appearing on the Dashboard.
*Outcome*: Additional improvement for Dashboard 2.0?

Current workaround is to add an additional Spokespeople relationship

messages.author: Chris Bonet
messages.text:
*User Need* - Archetype is looking to track organization mentions that are featured in quotes, not just who is quoted.
*User Problem -* I want to know when Microsoft Teams is mentioned in a quote by a Slack spokesperson.
*Outcome* - Further refines quote detection needs for clients.

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text:
User need: Folder level edits (I'd like to add a RLs to al the searches in a given folder in one go)
User Problem: I have to add a Reading List to all my Searches
Outcome: I dont have to edit many searches over and over again

messages.author: U01MR8JKSLE
messages.text:
*User need*: Improve the legacy feature of Quotes / Mention detection

*User problem*: This feature (from what I have been told) has not been worked on for while and may need some fine-tuning / updating to improve accuracy - clients often come to Support to flag in accuracies found from this particular feature.

*Outcome*: Possibly improvement for Dashboards 2.0

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
*User Need -* Heidrick & Struggles hopes the quality of translation (Particularly Korean, Japanese and Chinese) can be improved
*User Problem -* 1) They cannot use the email bulletin to share news with top management because of the translation (see image below). 2) they do not understand what the translated article means. 3) Coverage has been missing because of incorrect translation
*Outcome -* Better coverage detection and higher chance of getting them to renew / expand their Signal subscription

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
*User Need* - Bain would like to track coverage for hundreds of spokespeople and wonder if there is a quick way of doing it (And whether the system can support such a large amount of searches)
*User Problem -* It will take a long time to create searches one by one
*Outcome -* Time saving

messages.author: U01PP4TK6H0
messages.text:
Need: IG Group would like the legends in charts to be fully legible when downloaded (currently they get cut off)
Why: This will help their reporting hugely!

messages.author: U02G14PE4P8
messages.text: Hi CS friends, can you please let me know who has clients requested editing dashboard before. I am currently running a usability test of our first release and would like to get your help testing it before we make it available internally in Signal.  Many thanks :slightly_smiling_face:

messages.author: U02R13135BK
messages.text:
Need: Fitch would like empty sections to be added to their daily report. If the empty sections were left in, that segment would not be published.
Outcome: To speed things up, they can add and remove any sections everyday

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: Fitch would like to be able to move sections vs individual articles in reports

messages.author: U01PP4TK6H0
messages.text: Request from me: Group the active users together in newsadmin - easier to pull off a list for clients!

messages.author: U02M7G46PLZ
messages.text:
Need: Marble Global would like the ability to edit Sentiment in bulk.
Problem: They have 7000 articles in a search and someone has a task for sentiment correction for those articles. Currently, the only way of correcting them is one story at a time.
Outcome: Quicker way of correcting sentiment on articles instead of doing it one by one.

messages.author: U02M7G46PLZ
messages.text:
Need: MSL Group would like article URL links in Excel sheets to be clickable.
Problem: Currently, clients have to double-click the article URL cell in order to make it clickable and most of them are not aware of this and have to copy and paste the links in order to access them.
Outcome: Quicker way of accessing article URLs in Excel sheets.

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: WE Communications would like metadata available for TVEyes broadcast content: monthly viewership, Audience data...etc

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: WE Communications would like the ability to customize the format of alerts/reports. For instance, some alerts they want to remove the snippet and just send headlines...etc

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Abacus Insight would like to add metadata from sources we don't monitor to their dashboards ASAP

messages.author: Charlotte Dialdas
messages.text:
*From Luther Pendragon*:
Need:  It would be great if you could go back to where you were when tagging if you click through to an article or source.
Problem: The team spend a lot of time going through searches tagging coverage. If you scroll down through a lot of articles and then click through to a source, you lose your place and the page refreshes back to the most recent 20 articles. This can be incredibly frustrating if you have a lot of coverage to go through.
Outcome: Save a lot of time and improve user workflow and experience

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Abacus Insights would like the ability to make dashboards load with all media, rather than target media

need: Copperfield would like a quick way to pull full texts from a saved search/bulletin into a Word doc
problem: they're unable to have shareable text
outcome: allow users to pull full text via the platform e.g. with LN, Newsedge, full texts

Outcome: Better understanding of the marketplace and subsequent horizon scanning

Problem: eToro use us religiously for reporting and if we miss something that cannot retrospectively be added to the platform or be manually added to the dashboards, it messes with their reporting
Outcome: Accurate reporting and no issues with our big client :slightly_smiling_face:

Need: Gorillas would like to be able to pick their competitors' colours within the dashboard
Problem: Currently they have to export all of their data from the dashboard into Excel so that they can colour code their competitors according to their brand
Outcome: Save time and clearer reporting

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text: :point_up::skin-tone-4:

One important thing that needs to be addressed as soon as possible:

Following our latest updates on shared workspaces and NLA management, whenever any user (NLA permitted or not) creates a search in an NLA enabled workspace, that search is automatically turned on for NLA online.
This ends up in searches that shouldn’t have any NLA content having NLA online, without users even noticing.
This is especially important in global accounts. I just tried creating a search in the America workspace of <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/abb2e750-abe8-4171-829a-931433b06780/web|Black an

*Outcome*: Will save NEA time when sorting through CSV exports (from a saved search or a tag) and skip the extra step they currently use to remove syndicates in excel.

Could all the ESG related Topics be viewable in some way?

*Problem* - A lot of the time customers have direct licenses for paywalled publications and they are not able to add this coverage into their analytics as Signal doesn't monitor those sources

problem - we only have it for some NLA titles however this is a US client

*User Problem* -  Almost all, if not all, local language articles about them have been missed and they have become the worst performer in that market which isn’t true. It also skewed the data on the APAC regional level.
*Outcome* - More accurate search results hence avoid the chance of churning this client and the possibility to expand th

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text:
*User need:* Reader Seats should not be case sensitive when adding a reader to a bulletin or report.
*Problem*: I added a reader to my bulletin, but because i didn't write the email exactly the way that the Reader is configured in the People page (e.g Louis.Cointepas@ vs louis.cointepas@) the system didn't recognize the Reader, and added me as a recipient instead.
*Outcome*: Readers don't see the content they are supposed to :disappointed:

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text:
user need - Qualtrics would like salience to be on other features in the platform
problem - we only have salience on organizations. Qualtrics is often times mentioned in the byline of the text that's not scraped so they miss out on salience coverage in their dashboards. the workaround is custom tags however not desired
outcome - have salience on the article level as well

messages.author: UCHGHEX39
messages.text:
User Need: Commetric would love the ability to move around and delete graphs in the Dashaboard.
Problem: Dashboards are wonderful because their whole aim is to allow clients to personalise and choose the data they wish to focus on. But by  insisting that all graphs must be included in the dashboard clients are limited in the level of personalisation they can get.
Outcome: By letting clients to remove or move graphs we are giving them the power to fully personalise their experience

messages.author: Charlotte Dialdas
messages.text:
User Need - The ability to search by podcast sources (like you can with broadcast, online and print sources)
Problem - A client I am pitching podcasts to (Luther Pendragon) would like to be able to search for their clients' mentions within podcasts. Currently, they have to use keyword searching alongside their client organisation entities to narrow down the search and its hard for the client to find specific podcast mentions. The transcripts can be inaccurate too, making this more difficult

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9

messages.author: Paggie Leung

*User Problem* -  They can have a dozens or more searches under each folder. If they decided to exclude a search to all the searches, they will need to edit that individually on all the searches.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong

user need - AmFam would like full names in recipients in bulletins/reports

*User Need* - Fitch APAC says when they compile reports on Signal, they have to manually delete headers/sections where there are no stories from the template – as highlighted in the image below. *User Problem* -  Since they are generating reports on a daily basis, they need to spend a few minutes extra to remove those headers
*Outcome* - More convenient for clients who use Reports regularly. They won’t find our competitors e.g. Factiva better than us and it w

*User Need* - Watatawa APAC would like to have the option of having syndicated articles added to reports with hyperlinks rather than identical summaries. If this cannot be done automatically when users adding articles to reports, they hope they would be able to add links to text in the summary boxes
*User Problem* - The report looks clumsy when syndicated articles are added
*Outcome* - Clients can have cleaner reports and will make this functionality more attractive for them, hence encourage usage.

messages.author: U1NR8QYKB
messages.text:
*Userneed (internal) -* Be able to delete / deactivate all users and Bulletins with one click on NewsAdmin
*Problem -* At present, Product Analysts and CSMs need to deactivate an org and then deactivate all users to ensure that they can no longer log in and are no longer counted as active users. There is also the need to delete the workspace or go into the workspace individually to switch off the Bulletins to ensure they no longer send.
*Outcome -* Quicker and more efficient for Signal employees to manage ex-customers

*Problem*: Client is using the dashboard to track the mentions of key leadership spokespeople of competitors versus that of their own leadership. If we change the DB to the 'spokesperson' filter - this doesn't give the same SOV insights as the 'related organisation' filter. However, when using the competitor comparison - we are only able to compare against the key search powering the dashboard.
*Outcome:* Would be great to have a section that pulls through and combines the spokespeople of the target organisation to track against th

messages.author: U01MR8JKSLE
messages.text:
Problem: Currently not a feature

messages.author: U02R13135BK

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text:
*NewsAdmin Feedback:*
The Impersonate and Deactivate buttons are dangerously close to one another

Need - YOY comparison on the Dashboard
Outcome - improved Dashboards, better customer experience, less time spent

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
WE Communications would like to lower the social engagement follower threshold to 100 rather then 5,000. They said 80% of their reporting is basic coverage reports and so the clients would want a more accurate picture than just that 5,000+ base.

As an extension, they would like a filtering option to select whether you see 100+, 5,000+ or Verified twitter accounts. They said this would bring incredible value to those coverage reports

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3

Problem - Save the Children International are in charge of reporting on their mentions globally and they need to see a breakdown of coverage by every source location globally
Need - They'd  like to be able to see more than 20 source locations within the summary tab on the right hand side rather than be restricted to 20. Currently they have to export their coverage and use filters to see this full list
Outcome - It would save the team time and allow them to use the data directly from the platform

messages.author: U01PP4TK6H0
messages.text: Please can the platform stop auto-correcting the time to 24 hours?

messages.author: Melanie Downes
messages.text:
Custom Tagging - To be able to pull custom tagged missed articles into a search when we're using the similar types filter.
Problem - Client wants to see all of their coverage (including retroactively found missed articles) in one specific search - but this can't be done for the searches that are built with an and/or filter without creating a feeder feed (which then loses sentiment)
Outcome - Client can see all coverage within their key search and not have to go and look at separately created tags for their missing pieces

messages.author: U02D3GYHWQ7
messages.text:
user need:
1. the option to select multiple saves at once and could then drag them into different folders
2. option to duplicate searches to specific folder rather than these being duplicated to the bottom of the list
Problem: it takes a lot of time to copy and organise the searches
Outcome: smooth process of copying searches and organising them

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text:
user need - Bain would like to ingest email subscription based newsletters like Bloomberg Deals newsletters
problem - we only ingest the website content and not what subscribers may see
outcome - read and view newsletter content

messages.author: Stephani Pineda
messages.text:
User Need: The option to include tagging on syndicated articles on an individual level.

Problem: At the moment, when applying a tag onto a syndicated piece, it will apply the tag on all articles syndicated. Pegasystems are currently having issues with removing coverage that are syndicated from their searches, especially as they do not want to remove a source in full from the associated searches.

Outcome: The option to tag syndicated articles, without affecting other syndications.

messages.author: U06URNEH3

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
Would it be possible to train an entity with a custom tag as a rule? That way, if a client notices an article that was an entity miss, they can apply the tag and have it join the appropriate search without having to create a feeder feed build to count entity capture + tagged content.

If its possible, That would also be a great way to generate feedback for the entity training.  We could see what the entities with the  most tagged content are and then use the tagged content to inform the new training cycle

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text: Is there a reason that this may be possible sometimes but not all the time?

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
Need - Clarity PR would like to be able to 'select all' articles within a search all of the time for either tagging or adding to a report.
Problem - Currently, sometimes they are able to 'select all' which saves a lot of time (see photo below) but the majority of the time this option does not appear. This means that the customer has to select all from the 20 visible articles and scroll down to load 20 more articles which takes time.
Outcome - The client would save a lot of time when creating reports

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text:

Problem: Clients with Consumer Facing Products (read = Insurance Companies) are flooded with adverts about themselves and their competitors
Outcome 1: Clients that try and use exclusions end up creating a never ending string / query / feed in Signal that can eventually make our cluster fall over.
Outcome 2: Some clients cannot rely Broadcast data as for every relevant coverage piece, there are hundreds of adverts (for our European Friend, Liberty Insurance are advertising a lot during Sports Games suc

User Need; Ability to add missed articles to Dashboard.
Problem: Client cannot add other articles that Signal hasn't captured to Dashboard
Outcome: Clients cannot rely on our dashboards as much as we would hope. In this case, Avison Young would like the ability to add articles not captured by Signal to their Dashboards to that they can use our dashboard to report to the business.

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text:
User Need: Accurate Reach data

messages.author: Chrystal Wong

messages.author: UU5H8648Y

If an Analyst moves to a new firm they don't monitor - they dont need any further mentions but they DO need to access the data for while they were at the original firm. Ideally, they'd be able to 'turn off' an search, but keep it nested in the folder so they can use the comparison feature but only see previous data for the one search up to when it was turned off

*Outcome* - Whilst Luther understand the difficulties in translating transcripts, they currently use Kantar alongside us, solely for their foreign language broadcast (through TVEyes). They are hoping to move

They'd like to avoid false results where the keyword and competitor's name is mentioned, but actually not in a conjunction with one another = PROXIMITY.
*Problem -* searching for "Apple Inc." + "Headphones" can bring irrelevant results, not necessarily when Apple's headphones are mentioned.
*Outcome -* accurate results, easy to narrow to a specific area of client's and competitors coverage (especially helpful for consumer brands)

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8

*User Problem* - They need something simple and catchy to show to their management. They had word cloud when using Factiva and other tools and they loved that. Though Signal has AI topics / organisations / people detection, they think these have excluded some of the important keywords.
*Outcome* - 1) Clients can just download an image which the most associated keywords, which will save time.

messages.text: It would be great if excels could be uploaded into search terms, especially for keywords: one excel cell = one keyword. This would help save a ton of time building searches for clients, CS and support.

messages.text:
*User Need* - Rebecca Kalra from Avison Young, would like to be able to add missed / dropped articles that we have not harvested into the Signal platfrom
*User Problem* - Paywalls often block articles from being harvested and subsequently added to Signal so users are “missing” coverage when it comes to producing reports / dashboards giving their organisation and inaccurate representation of how many media hits they have had
*Outcome* - Users would have a far more accurate view of their media landscape and be able to report more accurately

*User Problem* - They are getting various names such as “Blackwitch” instead of “Black and Veatch” on Signal when the articles are in Chinese (and potentially other foreign languages). It looks bad when bulletins or reports are not showing their own company name correctly, plus they should have missed coverage because for this.
*Outcome* - The client will see higher quality of translated articles despite they do not master the foreign language(s), plus it will be unlikely for them

messages.author: UF907JYCF
messages.text:
<!here> Thank you so much for putting the feedback in the format <@U02G14PE4P8> suggested, this is really helpful for us to identify the problem areas.

We noticed that people have been upvoting certain pieces of feedback such as the dashboard setup feedback from Mabel. The product team have found this really useful for identifying the scale of a problem.

Moving forward, can everyone start to upvote :arrow_up: and comment on problems. This will help us spot emerging trends in the platform and will help us identifying people we might need to follow up with to solve the issues you're seeing.

T

messages.author: UCHGHEX39
messages.text:
Feedback from me: Dashboards are hard for clients to understand
Problem: During training sessions I am getting repeated questions to make Dashboards clearer. Clients are confused about how to make Dashboards or edit them. Is there a way that we can visually make searches for emails and searches for Dashboards different. Perhaps using an automated icon
Outcome: Signal is a SAAS tool. If the experience was easier clients could set up and edit Dashboards for themselves

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
*Need* - Mishcon de Reya would like to be able to search in Foreign Language
*Problem* - A lot of their clients are international and have non-English names. By translating the name of their clients into English, the translations are often dodgy and coverage is irrelevant or missed.
*Outcome* - They would love to be able to search in Foreign Language to avoid missing client coverage

messages.author: Luize Lazdane
messages.text: ^^ This feedback *just now* came from Michael at H&K as well. He would like to add the proximity metric into the search so that he can get relevant results only instead of any mentions. We will explore using saliency for now but that still runs the risk of returning quite a lot of irrelevant results.

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text:
*Need* - Mishcon de Reya would like to have a proximity metric to help when setting up complex organisation in relation to topic searches
*Problem* - Currently, they aren't using Signal as much as they would like to as topic searches are bringing through too much noise.
*Outcome* - They feel that introducing a proximity metric would filter out a lot of irrelevant coverage and insure the topic coverage was fully relevant to the organisation they are searching for, ultimately saving time

messages.author: U01MR8JKSLE
messages.text: Finn Partners would like to include a new graph within dashboards that shows SOV over time.

messages.text: Invesco Hong Kong flags that they are missing coverage because their company name hasn’t been translated correctly. We try to fix this by keyword searching the wrongly translated name, but this will bring other irrelevant mentions and they couldn’t detect sentiment accurately. They hope there is a back-end fix.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Edeleman Amsterdam would like the ability to customize font report templates

messages.author: Paggie Leung

messages.author: U02DBGKTDUN

*Outcome:* Automating this feature would save Lora lots of time, and advises that this would really add to her customer experience.

You will notice that on searches there is a new tab called ‘Topics’. If your search includes an organisation entity, you

_When I.... I want to... So that ..._

messages.author: U02G14PE4P8
messages.text:
Hi @all :wave:
I am Lu, the User experience designer recently joined the PR team. As the nature of my role, I am keen to understand our customers and their needs just like all of you. :-) This is a great channel of getting to know the customer feedbacks. And thank you for taking action of adding *WHY* especially for the feature request feedbacks.

The complication of the *feature only request* is that on the surface it sounds like a reasonable request. However, the product team can’t do a great job with the information they have. They could implement the feature in several different ways. And

messages.text: Ogilvy Public Relations would like to be able to see in the app if print articles are featured in the main paper, or the business section. why: Their client would like to know, as if an article is on one of the front pages of the paper it is important for them to know, and if it is the biz section it is less of a splash

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: McKinsey would like to see a wider article keyword/summary when there have been multiple mentions of entities / keywords. WHY: So they can ascertain relevancy more easily

"Adding a feature to allow people to search for two or more elements appearing close to each other (within 5/10 words) would massively improve the usability of the platform."

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8

Heidrick & Struggles APAC hopes Signal can:

messages.text:
Vivid Seats would like the ability to temporarily remove items in the soft filters on a dashboard in the same way you can click on them to zoom into particular details.

They would like this to function in the same way we add Signal lists to reading lists and are able to toggle individual sources on or off. They don’t want to permanently remove a search from a folder powering a dashboard.

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text: Apologies as I’m highlighting a problem and not offering any potential solution, but I feel I need to mention that our reach figures (especially the super high ones for some titles) often come under our clients’ scrutiny. Just this week, I had several conversations about this aspect of the reporting alone.

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T

Essentially, can we have the option to receive bulletins using the 'Most Relevant' functionality. This would definitely make the platform more consistent!

We have made some tests,

messages.author: Michal Semczyszyn
messages.text: To answer some of the concerns here. We are thinking of a solution where you can click on the title of any chart (Dashboard or insights) to go to a page and see a full width view of that chart with all of the items displayed (all bars, all - more than 10- segments of a pie chart, etc.). So the thinking is - Dashboard/Insights pages give you a snapshot of most important things (if we were to show all options on all charts at once it would), but you can drill into more detail view if you want to. These ‘full view’ pages could make it also easier to showcase important articles related to an graph

messages.author: Stephani Pineda

Jeff Cushing (<mailto:jcushing@we-worldwide.com|jcushing@we-worldwide.com>) from We Communications would like to:
• *Pin the Search-bar without having to constantly press ‘Edit Search’*  (“_It allows me to know what search terms are included (or left out) in case I want to tweak the search --which I often do” )_ 
• *When exporting results, have the specific search terms included* (_“This could either be in a designated cell (e.g., A:1 in Excel) or perhaps on a separate worksheet tab.“_)

messages.author: U01MR8JKSLE

University of Surrey, would like to be able to export individual articles.
This was once a feature, but is now unavailable.

messages.text: Haggie Partners has set up many bulletins and they need a button which allows them to pause all email bulletins for a particular person who goes on leave.

messages.author: UN05S2C7Q

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text: Abi and Lawrence from Harpswood would like to embed graphs within reports

messages.author: Sara Jacobs
messages.text: Rob Shrimpton FROM Babel PR - would like to have a Wordcloud (graph) in the platform

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
Feedback from Colliers China:
1. They strongly hope we would have the ability to display content in original languages in email bulletins and on the platform, due to translation quality and stakeholders’ preference
2. They also want the ability to search in local languages, because they fear the search won’t be precise enough when involving two-time translations 
3. They hope we could improve our China content

messages.author: U01M80VLY69
messages.text: TMF would like to be able to schedule bulletins to send monthly rather than being limited to weekly at most

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: WE Comms would like the date range in the dashboard view to remain set to the previously used range. (Similar to how searches work.) Dashboards currently reset to a 30 day view every time you open them.

messages.author: U1NR8QYKB
messages.text: Harry @ Nesta would like to be able to generate a reading list based on publication reach to ensure he is only looking at the biggest publication

messages.author: U01M80VLY69
messages.text: Feedback from me: It could be useful to have an EMEA source location rather than having to add in Europe, Western Asia etc each time for additions or exclusions.

messages.author: UF907JYCF
messages.text: On the above, we could also use this feature as a source feedback option and analyse what sources are having to be added the most by our clients and if we’re consistently missing articles from certain publications

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Pegasystems would like to add articles to the platform directly

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
Colliers China would like us to scan news from News Organisation’s mobile apps, rather than just websites especially when we do not have the print versions.

They also want to have the ability to add and tag missing stories in to the platform so that they can have the dashboards and insights graphs right.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text: Pega and Broadridge would like to add metadata into the platform for content that is not picked up by Signal AI. Specifically in the Dashboard view

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Can we remove reader licenses from showing up when searching for an organization in NewsAdmin? Ideally we should only see platform users in this view:

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: TMF Group would like the option to see news in chronological order. they're also spending a lot of time dragging articles in reports to reflect oldest to newest news

messages.author: Sara Jacobs
messages.text: Request from Fariha Rahman at Edelman Whatsapp - she would like to see sentiment data in Reports (per article)

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Requests from Fitch APAC:
• Offer older data which would at least allow them to do full year year-on-year comparison (e.g. 2020 vs 2021)  
• Create a topic for ESG

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text: Feedback on NewsAdmin: When I tick Monitor + Insight for an Organization, I'd like Dashboards and Sentiment to also be automatically enabled, and not have to tick those two boxes as well.

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text: Feedback from me: Custom tags should be applied to individual articles, not to all lead and syndicated articles. It is extremely cumbersome to filter out syndicated articles when a client only wants to see individual articles that are tagged.

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text:
Phyllis Cheng from Marsh Singapore (different person from the one Chrystal talked to above) would like to know if we will look into building the ability to download Dashboard images in one go. If yes, when we would be able to launch that functionality.

She hopes we can merge the “Top 20 publications” charts from four to one.

She expressed her concern on missing content from the Signal platform, which explains why she only gave us a 3 nps score.

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: BAT would like dashboard descriptions for each chart

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: Marsh APAC would like the ability to download Dashboard charts at once without having to download them individually

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text: Sean at Pegasystems would like the logo for each publication to be visible in :paperclip: curated reports :paperclip:

messages.author: U06URNEH3

messages.author: Chrystal Wong

messages.author: U01FSJ23NJX
messages.text: :arrow_up: bulk adding sources, merging reading lists and the above will make Support, Success, Sales and Clients’ lives better.

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: Please please please can we get an option of building a new reading list using an existing Organisation reading lists (rather than just Signal’s)? We have to use search functionality to combine several lists into one at the moment and using it as a feeder feed - not ideal!

messages.author: UU5H8648Y

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Pega run monthly & quarterly reports, being able to pull all the dashboard metrics into a PPT / PDF would be incredibly useful!

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T

messages.author: Stephani Pineda
messages.text:
Fully Vested PR would like to ensure the graphs found in Dashboards are fully readable, as keys for certain charts aren’t fully accessible.
Please see screenshot below:

messages.author: Michael Manley
messages.text: Marsh would also like the ability to swap which placement appears on the story card level. For example, Sally wanted the Reuter's article, which appeared in the also in section. She would like to ability to click and have the story card swap so she can then tag the placement

messages.author: U0107B2330B
messages.text: Tanaka Dzambo from H+K would love to be able to upload CVS files to create reading lists - Support would love that as well.

messages.author: UV7L9K3FF
messages.text:
Poss for next time- 
<https://predatech.co.uk/british-data-awards/>

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text: Canada Life would like to be able to do monthly/quarterly/ year on year comparisons in a side by side graph

messages.author: Chris Bonet
messages.text: Feedback from me: When a user is browsing a search, they should be able to add a source to a reading list from either the list of articles or the storycard section, similar to how the dropdown option when they are able to add individual articles to reports.

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text: Clyde & Co would like the date range in their search to save as a default to the one last used (full 15 months) - it is always going back to the last 24hrs at the moment

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
T. Rowe Price shared some frustration over the in-app behavior of 'Also-In' links. They would like the ability to click the also in link, and have it switch the story card to show that particular publication, rather than take you to the website.

They flag content for their newsletter and also their RSS feed in the platform, and they often prefer a publication in the also in links rather than the one prioritized on the story card. I let them know they can search the headline and the desired source as a workaround, but ultimately they want to be able to swap the story cards in real time

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T
messages.text: Freddie at Barts Health NHS Trust has asked if there is a way to download the Top 20 Publications from the graph in the Dashboard, at the moment it only provides the Top 5 which doesn't give the full picture.

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T
messages.text: Hi all, do you know if  bulletins can just pull through media coverage from the last 24 hours? ie if we had a bulletin set up daily for 8am we would only need to see content from 8am on the previous day, so over a 24 hour period

messages.author: U01FSJ23NJX
messages.text: Carl Alexander from <https://news-admin.tool.signal-ai.com/organisations/3d389130-3513-4655-b2eb-26fac8081ff7|St George's, University of London> thinks it would be great if there was a way to dig out older than 15 months data through ad-hoc requests when needed

messages.author: James Nott
messages.text: Likewise for The Green Party who were particularly interested in how they were perceived, and versus other political parties

messages.author: U01PP4TK6H0
messages.text: FIS are really excited about perception, perfect user case too! Their goal is to shift brand perception from a financial services company to an innovative tech first organisation.

messages.author: U01MR8JKSLE
messages.text: Evelien from O9 Solutions would like to have Word Clouds :cloud: as a feature

messages.author: Lucas Martino
messages.text:
Alex from Freetrade would like more flexibility in Dashboards. 
He uses them every morning with his team to chart progress vs. metrics like share of voice. 
Being able to see Coverage over time not in amount of articles but in % would be very useful.

messages.author: Rosie Crouch
messages.text: Feedback from me - it would be awesome if we could assign users to workspaces on newsadmin

messages.author: U01Q1Q2HTNV
messages.text: Feedback from me - it would be good if you could reorder the dashboards like you can with saved searches

messages.author: UL7666P28
messages.text: Feedback from me: It would be useful if the story card of an article displayed the media type.

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text: CISI would like to see the global heat map (seen in insights) within dashboards

messages.text: Hi Team, Cult wines would like to see the % of topic coverage for each of their competitors on the dashboard (rather than needing to “eyeball” it) - currently volume of articles is shown

messages.author: Louis Cointepas
messages.text: UX Feedback: When I am impersonating and creating searches, the _Save your Search_ button can easily be obscured by the drop down options that sit under the impersonation icon

messages.author: U01UQ5G678T
messages.text: I am sure this has already been covered previously but I have had a number of clients asking if there is an option to download the whole dashboard interface into 1 presentable template rather than a user having to download the CSV file or PDF for each graph individually

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Sabre Singapore hopes we can have “Asia Pacific” as a source location or  location option rather than “Asia” plus “Oceania”.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Belmond and our support team will benefit from the ability of adding client’s reading lists on other reading lists. For example, Belmond asked us to build about a dozen top media list for different markets. They now want a master reading list to include all of them and the support team is helping by adding those titles one by one.

Though they can potentially use “Feeder Feeds” or add those reading lists one by one to a search, they definitely find having a master list much clearer and less confusing.

messages.author: U010QTF9AHF
messages.text: :slightly_smiling_face:

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text: Both Jim and Melina who curate a Weekly Report at Black & Veatch are very complimentary of our UX: They both raved about how easy it was compared to their previous vendor

messages.author: U06URNEH3
messages.text:
Patrick at Black & Veatch would like to share the dashboards with fewer clicks (one click?) rather than have to download the individual widgets one by one.

He is also open for some user research ahead of dashboards pro should the UX team be interested

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: World Bank would like to see more than a 15 month archive as they do quarter on quarter comparisons

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Feedback from me based on our experience with Ashurst: Would be good if clients can duplicate or copy any Reports from one workspace to another.

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text: CPT would like to remove duplicated stories from their searches so that they only see the Lead Story

messages.text: <@UU5H8648Y> The compare to previous period would be a great feature.

messages.text:
Feature Requests from 1Password:

2. Will would like our website to have a 'Support' page with all of our one pagers and video tutorials. He is a HUGE tech guy and wants to know everything about the platforms capabilities, therefore having to search for a specific guide in the intercom chat limits to knowledge to what he knows to search for. They have a simi

messages.text: Feedback from me - it would be good if when you're creating a dashboard the relationship name auto populates when you add new relationships (like when you first open the form)

messages.author: UL7666P28

messages.text: ahh GINA

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Fitch Hong Kong would benefit from the ability to duplicate reading lists themselves.

messages.author: Paggie Leung

He said Vuelio allows that. Without this ability he couldn’t generate graphs from Signal because a lot of Australian titles are heavily paywalled and Signal doesn’t have access.

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text:
Marsh Singapore would like us to:
1. Offer pre-built media lists on Signal based on National Newspapers, TV Broadcasters, Trade Publications, Online News Portal based for each of the Asian countries, instead of us asking them to give us media lists to build for them. Currently we have a few only and some need updating 
2. Allow downloading all Dashboard graphs with one click or incorporate charts in the dashboard to the daily bulletin
3. Include trending topics 
4. Expand our content coverage to offer more paywalled content and broadcast TV channels

messages.author: UHHS93FKP
messages.text:
Morning. Is there any further feedback on our attempts (with partners) to remove advertising from our surfaced content? The issue remains a major bugbear for eToro compromising their reporting, and there is a call this afternoon where this has been tabled as an agenda point, noting they have been raising it for over 12 months.
Many thanks.

messages.author: Chris Bonet
messages.text: Gina Simonis at Invesco would like the following Summary section in Comparisons to be foldable ala when viewing Articles. She is hoping this will help the resolution of the Comparisons charts on the left.

messages.text: Similar to Paggie's request above HDR UK would like to be able to send dashboards within email bulletins

messages.author: U01FSJ23NJX
messages.text:
From me: it’d be great to have a Media type filter (as we have a Sentiment filter) as a standard option on Dashboards. Now if the clients want to filter by Media type we need to create a search for each type, which is not good for Elasticsearch.
As an example: Belmond wants this filter in over 30 Dashboards across 11 workspaces

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

messages.author: UL7666P28

They'd also love for it to be on the dashboard as an average score they can benchmark from and report back to the business overtime to measure success

messages.author: U01FSJ23NJX

Signal Ambassador Benjamin Thiele-Long from Cognito would like to have a way to cap how many stories surface in each group within bulletins.
i.e. 5/10/20 articles per section

messages.author: U01PP4TK6H0

messages.author: U01Q1Q2HTNV

messages.author: Jessica Huggins

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

It seems as atm you would need to do it for each of the entity separately.

messages.author: U0107B2330B
messages.text: Sara @ signal It would be great if we could filter on all topics in a dashboard similar to selecting Target Media in one go

messages.author: U1NR8QYKB
messages.text: Callum @ Signal, it would be great if we could filter by time as well as date. When locating broadcast clips you often have to keep scrolling to find clips from the morning of a certain day, hourly filtering would be great, for example between 10am - 11am

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9
messages.text: Hunt & Gather US would like to pull tweets from specific journalists

messages.author: Lucas Martino
messages.text:
Feedback from me that I’m sure has been flagged before: it’s really important that we find a way to add tagged articles to a search without having to use a feeder.
Right now, if an article is missed by the entity and the search is set to similar types (because it includes keywords, different source locations, etc), we need to put the whole search in a feeder to surface that tagged article without destroying the original search, which means that we’ll loose sentiment.

It’d be amazing if we could simply add tagged articles to a search without having to change it completely. Easier for us to bui

messages.author: U01M80VLY69
messages.text: Red Flag Consultancy would like to be able to add more than 10 feeds in the Comparison tab

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text: It would be great to have the filter of “Headline / Summary / Body”. Headline & summary is not as helpful, especially for the partners use

messages.author: UN05S2C7Q
messages.text: The 'duplicate folders within a workspace' feature is a lifesaver, thank you :heart:

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: Prosek would like the ability to download text to PDF without having to do so manually

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Colliers Singapore will benefit from the ability to add their own reading lists to a reading list (Currently we can only add Signal Lists). This would help save a lot of the Support Team’s time building lists too.

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
Was just on a call with Anaplan as they wanted me to walk  their VP of Corporate Marketing and others trough our platform. On the call, the Senior Director of Public Relations said "This is not a typical monitoring and reporting platform.... this is a *Decision Driving platform*"

What a win!

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8

They also hope we can expand our China sources - including those free online websites.

It’d be amazing to be able to choose what scale the graphs are using. Quite often it happens that one of the tracked entities has a larger amount of mentions than the rest, making the graph completely useless. See below as an example

"I also have a question about monitoring. This blog post on <https://blog.businesswire.com/keeping-it-real-using-pr-to-combat-misinformation-and-disinformation?utm_source=bwteam&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=fy21q2-disinformation-wp&utm_content=pr-tips-to-stop-fake-news|Business Wire highlights> how to combat both mis- and disinformation you need to be listening, and it notes that it needs to be done at the dark web level, as that’s where a lot of it starts:
_Start listening. Disinformation typically originates “in the fringes of the internet or on t

Carly from Experian:

Well done all!

Also included in this, they would like the option to download the signal links OR the original links

messages.author: UB0QCCUCU
messages.text:
It would be great if, when creating a reading list, the reading list knows what titles are in it already. If you have a title which is in a reading list IN a reading list, and then you try to add that title, it comes up as ‘not in list’, which isn’t true! It seems like when a reading list is in a reading list, Signal can’t ‘see’ the titles. I hope the pictures attached help explain myself!

The reason this is an issue is that it can duplicate workload when trying to put together reading lists in Signal. It can also cause issues on large reading lists where you aren’t sure if a title is actuall

messages.author: U0107B2330B
messages.text: Vienn McMasters from EY would us to look at the quotes/mentions anlysis as some of the "mentions" write this person comments which should be a classified a quote.

messages.author: UFKN4813N
messages.text: L’Oreal: For broadcast, it is incredibly difficult to parse out ad campaigns, individual ads, and promotions. The topics that we have set up don’t affect search volumes, and the feeds start to become noisy and less efficient. Really tough place to be when we need to surface impactful broadcast coverage, and we’re seeing daytime tv

messages.author: Joanna Cresswell
messages.text: Cornerstone Research would like to be able to receive email bulletins with the key topics/themes derived from the specific search - for example what are the key themes/topics that their competitors were mentioned about in the past hour/day/week and so on. Hope I’ve explained it well?

messages.author: U0107B2330B

messages.author: Amelia Fuell
messages.text: feedback from me - Would be great to be able to add sections to bulletins (ie a header), as we can in reports? It's such a small feature but i feel it would make longer bulletins look much more presentable

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text: Can we add to the intercom a way for clients to request topics/merge with the entity request? I find that more often than not, clients have great ideas for topics they’re not seeing in the platform, and often forget what they are when we have conversations

This applies to all the Chinese-language articles about this company and also the curated newsletter MM prepared for them - and they are not too happy with this.

messages.author: UKF5PD0U9

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ
messages.text: It would be great for client to have an option to flag unindexed sources - this is something that shouldn’t really be happening, but when it is it would be great to have a pop up of some sort “is your source not displaying?” - otherwise they end up excluding a phrase and get frustrated when coverage is still coming through.

messages.author: U013PDVG8TS
messages.text: (They would like the flexibility to amend their colour on Dashboards to the light green, rather than the dark blue). <@U01P8CLFLRM> fyi

messages.author: Amelia Fuell
messages.text: "Now the real fun starts and we cannot wait to make full use of this. And, if we could at some stage get our Insights chart colour as the green, even better :slightly_smiling_face: "

messages.author: U013PDVG8TS
messages.text: From McDermott Will & Emerry, who signed this week:

messages.author: UN05S2C7Q

messages.author: U0107B2330B

messages.author: Fabian Cornwell

Users often complain about not being able to preempt certain NLA pieces in their saved searches or if setting up a new search not being able to access the most important publications. Unsure if this is compliant or not!

messages.author: Paggie Leung
messages.text: Marsh Australia says we are missing some key print Australian publications and hope we can expand our Australian media coverage (though they have NewsEdge already). With the print version always very different with the online ones, they would like to have both available on the platform.

messages.author: UFJ2LCR32
messages.text: Clients (specifically Data & Insights users, Analyst users who use Signal directly) would like the ability to create searches that include 'Sentiment' (as well as Salience) as search term/filter or the ability to further refine searches by sentiment or salience using the soft filters. In addition, when sentiment is manually updated on Signal, it would be great if there was a way to keep track of which articles have been manually updated (similar to when an article is added to a report - the right corner of the article turns green).

messages.author: U1NR8QYKB
messages.text: Sanoobar Patel @ Peabody would like the ability to add articles to a search when they are dropped / missed so they have a record for their reporting

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

messages.text: Hi guys, I am not sure if this was previously mentioned by CSMs/sales, but it would be super helpful if clients could only search for and use (in their organisation reading lists and searches) the publications that are covered by the content in their subscription - it would be amazing to show them the others available through Signal perhaps as “greyed out”option, avoid any confusions during and post trial and make things very transparent

messages.author: Isobel Hillebrandt
messages.text: Hill + Knowlton Strategies (UK) would like to be able to create searches using sentiment as a filter

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

messages.author: UU2K3HLRZ

messages.author: Mabel Warnock
messages.text: Feedback from Hudson Sandler: While they loved dashboards they found it really weird that deleting a search did not delete the corresponding Dashboard. Its very hard to delete a dashboard if the corresponding search is gone. You actually have to make a whole new, fake dashboard in order to get rid of it

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text: Feedback from me: when toggling dashboards by one topic (i.e. COVID vaccines), that topic should not then appear within the key topics area, as it’s redundant. I then want to see only the topics that coincide with the selected topic - COVID vaccines - I’m toggling for. Does that make sense?

messages.author: UFJ2LCR32
messages.text: Hi Guys, it would be great if the client user can copy searches themselves in a Workspace (or within their own environment). For instance, easier to copy searches and then tweak Organisations in each search rather than create the similar search over again (multiple times sometimes if they are doing a like for like comparison of something like an Org or Topic etc). Currently, clients have to request this via Support or Success.

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Lovely feedback from Emirates / Dnata’s Gabor: Signal is a game changer and I can only give you positive feedback on the platform. It is so easy to use and the bulletins are helping me so much in monitoring news.

messages.author: UHJMCDDL3
messages.text: ^ I would find this really useful too

messages.author: UQD88CTQ8
messages.text: Feedback from me on Dashboard: can we have the total number of coverage shown on the dashboards? Right now clients will need to do the math from either the figures showing on the sentiment graph or other charts to get the total. Thank you!

messages.author: UF907JYCF
messages.text: Feedback from me: If we send a bulletin with a link to a clip can we also add a link back to the clip in the platform? Clients often get confused when we send just the transcript and would love to view the actual clip

messages.author: UU5H8648Y
messages.text:
From the Editorial Director at DropBox:

"just a quick note to let you know i really appreciate all of what you've done to set us up for success with signal. i was asked yesterday to pull some baseline metric for 2020 to use for 2021 KPIs and narrative planning. with the dashboards all set up the way they are, i was able to convincingly fulfill the request in about an hour and everyone was very pleased.

the downloadable pngs also worked really well, so i was able to mix your graphics with simple tables of specific data values, and i felt confident that the data was actually showing something

messages.author: UD5PWRWMC
messages.text: Thanks for the feedback. This looks to have come up a couple of times recently. I'd like to understand what they're trying to do a little better. Is it just so they can make sure they're monitoring the correct spokespeople? Or is there something more they're trying to do?

messages.author: U02K7HETZ

messages.author: U06URNEH3

Feedback from Marsh Australia:

messages.author: UFKN4813N

T. Rowe Price feedback:
Dashboards are fantastic, but they need to report out the raw data from the Dboards and it’s complicated to get the articles that power the graphics.

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
Feedback from L’Oreal:
“When are you going to get the Reach thing fixed? It is really skewing our numbers and we can’t report out to senior management from the platform because of this.”

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
Feedback from Skadden:
1. Sometimes the same individual is prompted to authenticate LN numerous times. They are using the correct browsers, troubleshooting, etc.
2. They are interested in a social listening tool being integrated into Signal - social virality would not solve for this
3. I shared with support already, but there is a lot of application time out errors happening which is really frustrating the team. (It’s not a one time or a one individual issue)

messages.author: UU5H8648Y

I showed Bank of America dashboards today, some key quotes were "I'm being impressed" "I could spend hours looking at this"

They loved it! They said a 'reader' view they could share would be a game changer and cut down on time they currently spend making reports.

messages.author: UFKN4813N
messages.text: Can we reorganize the magnifying glass dropdown by most used filters versus the way it is now? I.e. when I build a search, more often than not, I use an entity, a reading list, a topic, and then a source location. Currently, reading lists have been bumped down quite a bit

messages.author: Sara Jacobs
messages.text: Congrats on the new added entities - I find that aesthetically the new symbols are slightly "harsher/darker" than the previous ones the old ones might need a review?  Also the order in the new search dropdown could perhaps be a bit more organised.  Either sorted alphabetically. Or the most used first : organisation / source location / source / reading lists / topics than rest.

messages.author: UEHKXVBA5

We’re planning to expand our digital PR/digital marketing services this year and social listening will inevitably become a bigger need. Meltwater popped up again list week to pitch their social wares via email and we know of other tools on the market. We seem to recall you had something in the works – is there any news?

We’re running into issues where internally, in Newsadmin, people will set a temporary password in Signal but forget to save the account. This is leading to unnecessary sign in issues, especially on our Partner accounts.

Is it possible to automatically save the user after setting a new temporary password? Or to prompt the newsadmin user to save the Signal user after setting a new pw?

I don't see this as a problem for clients as they will unlikely be managing workspaces all that frequently (it's possibly more likely for Support and Success) but I just wanted to flag in case this wasn't known

Keep it up

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
The World Bank has noted that while we count the number of keywords / search term mentions in the platform (in parentheses like so 4x..) they would like to see the number of mentions in the Excel. To be clear, this is not related to AI topics, but rather manual keywords they use in searches.

This has also been requested by a number of my measurement/evaluation clients, like Prosek, Archetype, and so on, as this provides a valuable way for them to measure how much a key term has penetrated coverage. They can then segment out coverage that didn't discuss something easily in the Excel. (Goal of

messages.author: U80K4EKT4

If you face the same issue: 1) adjust the name of the search i was using to be less characters and/or 2) remove a topic or two from the folder powering this chart and then refresh. Unfortunately means you may have to have 2 charts to display everything you’re wanting to see.

Product are aware but workaround was shared on Sales Feedback so posting here

messages.author: U80K4EKT4
messages.text:
_*SAVED SEARCHES FEEDBACK:*_

With the deployment of dashboards, I am increasingly seeing more folders with different use cases. Whether it's bulletins, reports, dashboard, insight/comparison, it's likely that all these folders (and respective searches) require different set ups. For example, Comparison graphs for SOV will include the company itself, whereas a Competitors folder under dashboards will not.

*(Almost) all Signal outputs are powered by saved searches, it can be very confusing or messy navigating these.* (This is compounded by my previous point above about Interests). As a result,

messages.author: U80K4EKT4
messages.text:
_*Are we planning to phase out Interests now?*_ 

Either way, there are some accounts (e.g. HSBC) where there are many Interests set up and they just overwhelm the account and give people access to a feature that I would recommend that they do not use. *It would be great, if Interests could be hidden/collapsed/removed from workspaces where they will not be needed.* Maybe feeds can be prioritised above them?

messages.author: Sara Jacobs

messages.author: UFKN4813N
messages.text: I would like to be able to have one source tagged into several different source locations. Reason being is sometimes one source (ex: Global Legal Chronicle) has offices in North America, EMEA, APAC, etc. but having  be tagged to one source location alone of Italy means it won't come through with a US source filter. This can mess up searches and put into question the validity of the data we are sharing with our clients and stop relevant articles from coming into important feeds. The ability to tag the one source to multiple countries will help in the long run.

How does L'oreal determine which source was the first to publish an article?

messages.author: U06URNEH3

messages.author: UFKN4813N
messages.text: L'oreal is noting that our syndication indicator is not accurate. AKA the lead story that we note is not the actual story originator. So when we note that an article is syndication, many times we list the original source as syndication, giving weight more toward the source that has more reach (i.e. Popsugar originated an article but in Signal we noted Yahoo as the lead). This adds time to her workflow to go in and correct this.

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
General UI/UX feedback from the full Prosek team from my meeting on Friday:
Signal is easy to use when you know how to use it. I.e. if you have a training or a walk through it's very easy to replicate that moving forward. Without training, just logging in, it's hard to know what you're looking at, how to begin a search, etc.

messages.author: U80K4EKT4

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
Ameriprise has flagged that they would like online video pieces to be tagged as Broadcast. Case and point: Bloomberg will post a lot of their broadcast content online and we show it as online news, when in fact it's video news. The reason why they would like this to happen is because those broadcast pieces _*aren't*_ being picked up by TVEyes, and as such their broadcast proactive outreach numbers are incorrect, simply based off of our media type classifications.
*End goal:* when an online news sources posts an online video piece, could we please look into a way tag it as broadcast? This affec

messages.author: Alex Kaufman
messages.text:
Feedback from Dimensional AND Prosek: when one goes into the comparison tab, and selects several competitors, the number of total articles doesn't change. It's counterintuitive because the graphs change and the desired behavior is to have a visualization of the selected data as well as a number to get a sense for how much coverage those specific competitors were bringing in separately.
I too agree with the feedback, and a number of other clients I've offered trainings to have mentioned the same thing.

messages.text: Hi All, we shared this Premium Social feedback previously but raising it here again because it's becoming a huge challenge for us on the KPMG Engine Room account. Every time KPMG need to set up a traditional media Bulletin for stories/issues they want to monitor, they also ask for a corresponding Premium Social alert. KPMG deals with crisis on a daily basis. The 10 Premium Social alert maximum per Workspace limitation is causing a lot of confusion (between Client and Signal), disorganisation, and quite counterproductive if we want the client to continue adopting the Premium Social offering. Yo

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: with ambiguous spokespeople names, it's difficult to know who's associated with which org. if this can be specified within the dropdown, it'd be super helpful

messages.author: Chelsea Bonhotal
messages.text: I’m trying to create a custom logic search with multiple AND and OR groups, but it seems like I’m limited to only AND or only OR groups in a given search. For this search, the client wants to see all mentions of F5 OR spokespeople mentions and have the entire search filtered by their priority reading list. I may be missing something but I can’t seem to figure out how to do this in just one search without using a feeder. Are there any plans to make custom logic more flexible? In order to create the search I’m aiming for, it seems like I’m going to have to create two feeder searches instead.

messages.author: Chrystal Wong
messages.text: S&P would like to be able to see top tier coverage without needing an exhaustive reading list as they don't want to miss relevant coverage. the suggested workaround is to filter by reach within the platform but that may not be the most accurate option

messages.author: Jermaine Roberts
messages.text: Feedback from me: understand it's not in the immediate scope, but any consideration to the ability to bulk edit colors on dashboards?  A cleaner way to reflect Competitor A = Red, Competitor B = blue, without editing multiple charts individually.
